```python
import re

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    extracted_info = []

    # Extract agent's absolute position
    agent_x, agent_y = None, None
    # Use re.DOTALL to allow '.' to match newlines, and \s* for flexible whitespace matching
    agent_pos_match = re.search(r"cursor:\s*(?:Yourself a archeologist)?\s*\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    if agent_pos_match:
        agent_x = int(agent_pos_match.group(1))
        agent_y = int(agent_pos_match.group(2))
        extracted_info.append(f"Agent_absolute_position: ({agent_x}, {agent_y}).")

    # Extract map information if agent position is known and map section exists
    map_start_idx = observation_text.find("map:\n")
    if agent_x is not None and agent_y is not None and map_start_idx != -1: # Ensure map section exists
        map_content_after_tag = observation_text[map_start_idx + len("map:\n"):]

        # Define markers for the end of the map content
        map_end_marker_1 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nAgent the Digger")
        map_end_marker_2 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nSt:") # Fallback for stat line

        map_end_offset = -1
        if map_end_marker_1 != -1 and (map_end_marker_2 == -1 or map_end_marker_1 < map_end_marker_2):
            map_end_offset = map_end_marker_1
        elif map_end_marker_2 != -1:
            map_end_offset = map_end_marker_2
        
        map_section_raw = ""
        if map_end_offset != -1:
            map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag[:map_end_offset].strip('\n')
        else:
            # If no clear end marker, take up to the next non-map section like "message:", "cursor:", or EOF.
            # This handles cases where map is the last or only big block.
            next_section_start = len(map_content_after_tag)
            
            # Look for subsequent main sections after the current map_start_idx
            # Consider this carefully to not prematurely cut if map has these words
            # For simplicity for now, stick to original end markers, if fails, it won't add map items.
            pass # Keep previous logic for map_section_raw, as current example shows proper endings.

        if map_section_raw:
            map_lines = map_section_raw.split('\n')
            
            # Find the agent symbol '@' on the parsed map_lines (which now include original padding)
            visual_agent_x_on_map, visual_agent_y_on_map = -1, -1
            for r_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                if '@' in line:
                    visual_agent_x_on_map = line.find('@')
                    visual_agent_y_on_map = r_idx
                    break
            
            if visual_agent_x_on_map != -1 and visual_agent_y_on_map != -1:
                # Calculate the offset between visual coordinates and absolute coordinates
                offset_x = agent_x - visual_agent_x_on_map
                offset_y = agent_y - visual_agent_y_on_map

                walls = []
                open_floor = []
                molten_lava = [] 
                fountains = []
                stairs_up = []
                
                for y_offset, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                    for x_offset, char in enumerate(line):
                        abs_x = x_offset + offset_x
                        abs_y = y_offset + offset_y

                        if char == '|':
                            walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'vertical'))
                        elif char == '-':
                            walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'horizontal'))
                        elif char == '.':
                            open_floor.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                        elif char == '}': # Classified as molten lava based on world knowledge and policy
                            molten_lava.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                        elif char == 'F': # Explicitly identify 'F' as fountains
                            fountains.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                        elif char == '<': # Explicitly identify '<' as stairs up
                            stairs_up.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                        # Add more symbols and their interpretations as needed

                if walls:
                    extracted_info.append(f"Walls observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(walls)))}.")
                if open_floor:
                    extracted_info.append(f"Open floor observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(open_floor)))}.")
                if molten_lava: # Report molten lava
                    # Molten lava is inherently lethal and impassable, so combine the label to avoid redundancy
                    extracted_info.append(f"Molten lava (lethal, impassable) observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(molten_lava)))}.")
                if fountains:    # Report fountains
                    extracted_info.append(f"Fountains observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(fountains)))}.")
                if stairs_up:    # Report stairs up
                    extracted_info.append(f"Stairs up observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_up)))}.")

    # Extract inventory items and general game messages
    # Refined regex to capture content strictly between "message:\n" and the next known section or end
    message_section_match = re.search(r"message:\n([\s\S]+?)(?=\n\n(?:cursor:|map:|Agent the Digger|St:|$))", observation_text)
    message_content = ""
    if message_section_match:
        message_content = message_section_match.group(1).strip()
    
    inventory_items_found = []
    # Regex to find individual inventory items: starts with a letter, then ' - ', then the item description.
    # It now uses finditer to get all matches and explicitly non-greedily matches the item description
    # until the start of another item or end of string.
    # Updated to ensure correct parsing when there are multiple items on a single line or mixed with other messages
    # Added a more specific negative lookahead to correctly terminate an item description
    items_matches = re.finditer(r"([a-z]) - ([^.\n]+?)(?=\.  [a-z] - |\.$|\Z|\n[a-z] - )", message_content)
    
    # Collect items first
    for match in items_matches:
        inventory_items_found.append(f"Inventory item '{match.group(1)}': {match.group(2).strip()}.")

    # If inventory items were found, add them to extracted_info
    if inventory_items_found:
        extracted_info.extend(inventory_items_found)
        # Remove inventory lines from message_content to isolate actual game messages
        for item_line in inventory_items_found:
            # Reconstruct the matched string to replace it effectively
            item_char_and_desc_match = re.search(r"Inventory item '([a-z])': (.+)\.", item_line)
            if item_char_and_desc_match:
                # The original pattern in the message was "j - a horn."
                original_pattern = f"{item_char_and_desc_match.group(1)} - {item_char_and_desc_match.group(2)}"
                message_content = message_content.replace(original_pattern, "").strip()
        
        # Remove any remaining " . " or " .  " separators after removing items
        message_content = re.sub(r'\s*\.\s*(?=\s*[a-z] - |$)', '', message_content).strip()
        message_content = re.sub(r'\s*\.\s{2,}', '', message_content).strip() # clean up multiple spaces after dot

    # Add remaining message content as a general game message, if any valid content remains
    if message_content and not re.match(r"^\s*$", message_content):
        # Only add if it doesn't already capture the cursor info, if no absolute position was found
        # (meaning the 'cursor' block might not have been fully parsed yet).
        # This check should ideally be redundant with better message matching
        if not re.search(r"cursor:\s*\(x=\d+, y=\d+\)", message_content):
            extracted_info.append(f"Game_message: {message_content}.")
        
    # Edge case from step 4 - "cursor: Yourself a archeologist (x=29, y=9)" was still showing up in message.
    # This specifically targets cases where a "cursor:" line without a preceding newline is caught.
    # Further refinement: The main message regex should really capture this correctly.
    # It appears my message_section_match regex `(?=\n\n(?:cursor:|map:|Agent the Digger|St:|$))`
    # correctly stops *before* a new section starting on a new line. But if "cursor:" is *within*
    # the message content itself (e.g., if there are no double newlines before it, as in step 4 example),
    # it gets included.
    # I'll strip any cursor lines from the *start* of the message content more aggressively if it accidentally included it.
    if extracted_info and extracted_info[-1].startswith("Game_message:"):
        game_msg = extracted_info[-1]
        cursor_in_msg = re.search(r"cursor:\s*(?:Yourself a archeologist)?\s*\(x=\d+, y=\d+\)\s*", game_msg)
        if cursor_in_msg:
            cleaned_msg = re.sub(r"cursor:\s*(?:Yourself a archeologist)?\s*\(x=\d+, y=\d+\)\s*", "", game_msg).strip()
            # If the entire message was just the cursor line, remove the message entirely.
            if not cleaned_msg.replace("Game_message: ", "").strip():
                extracted_info.pop()
            else:
                extracted_info[-1] = cleaned_info[-1].replace(cursor_in_msg.group(0), "").strip()
                if extracted_info[-1].endswith("."): # Ensure single period at end of message.
                    extracted_info[-1] += ''
                else:
                    extracted_info[-1] += '.'

    # Extract agent stats
    stats_match = re.search(
        r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",
        observation_text
    )
    if stats_match:
        dlvl, gold, current_hp, max_hp, current_pw, max_pw, ac, current_xp, next_level_xp = stats_match.groups()
        extracted_info.append(
            f"Agent_stats: Dlvl:{dlvl}, Gold:{gold}, HP:{current_hp}/{max_hp}, Pw:{current_pw}/{max_pw}, AC:{ac}, Xp:{current_xp}/{next_level_xp}."
        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)
```

import re

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    extracted_info = []

    # Extract agent's absolute position
    agent_x, agent_y = None, None
    # Use re.DOTALL to allow '.' to match newlines, and \s* for flexible whitespace matching
    agent_pos_match = re.search(r"cursor:\s*(?:Yourself a archeologist)?\s*\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    if agent_pos_match:
        agent_x = int(agent_pos_match.group(1))
        agent_y = int(agent_pos_match.group(2))
        extracted_info.append(f"Agent_absolute_position: ({agent_x}, {agent_y}).")

    # Extract map information if agent position is known
    # This logic block should only run if there is a 'map:' section
    map_start_idx = observation_text.find("map:\n")
    if map_start_idx != -1 and agent_x is not None and agent_y is not None:
        map_content_after_tag = observation_text[map_start_idx + len("map:\n"):]

        # Define markers for the end of the map content
        map_end_marker_1 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nAgent the Digger")
        map_end_marker_2 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nSt:") # Fallback for stat line
        map_end_marker_3 = map_content_after_tag.find("\n\n") # Generic double newline for safety

        map_end_offset = -1
        if map_end_marker_1 != -1 and (map_end_marker_2 == -1 or map_end_marker_1 < map_end_marker_2):
            map_end_offset = map_end_marker_1
        elif map_end_marker_2 != -1:
            map_end_offset = map_end_marker_2
        elif map_end_marker_3 != -1:
             map_end_offset = map_end_marker_3
        
        map_section_raw = ""
        if map_end_offset != -1:
            map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag[:map_end_offset].strip('\n')
        else:
            # If no clear end marker, take the rest of the map_content_after_tag
            map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag.strip('\n')

        if map_section_raw:
            map_lines = map_section_raw.split('\n')
            
            # Find the agent symbol '@' on the parsed map_lines (which now include original padding)
            visual_agent_x_on_map, visual_agent_y_on_map = -1, -1
            for r_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                if '@' in line:
                    visual_agent_x_on_map = line.find('@')
                    visual_agent_y_on_map = r_idx
                    break
            
            if visual_agent_x_on_map != -1 and visual_agent_y_on_map != -1:
                # Calculate the offset between visual coordinates and absolute coordinates
                offset_x = agent_x - visual_agent_x_on_map
                offset_y = agent_y - visual_agent_y_on_map

                walls = []
                open_floor = []
                molten_lava = [] 
                fountains = []
                stairs_up = []
                
                for y_offset, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                    for x_offset, char in enumerate(line):
                        abs_x = x_offset + offset_x
                        abs_y = y_offset + offset_y

                        if char == '|':
                            walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'vertical'))
                        elif char == '-':
                            walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'horizontal'))
                        elif char == '.':
                            open_floor.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                        elif char == '}': 
                            molten_lava.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                        elif char == 'F': 
                            fountains.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                        elif char == '<': 
                            stairs_up.append((abs_x, abs_y))

                if walls:
                    extracted_info.append(f"Walls observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(walls)))}.")
                if open_floor:
                    extracted_info.append(f"Open floor observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(open_floor)))}.")
                if molten_lava:
                    extracted_info.append(f"Molten lava (lethal, impassable) observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(molten_lava)))}.")
                if fountains:    
                    extracted_info.append(f"Fountains observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(fountains)))}.")
                if stairs_up:    
                    extracted_info.append(f"Stairs up observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_up)))}.")

    # Extract inventory items and general game messages
    message_match = re.search(r"message:\n([\s\S]+?)(?=(?:\n\ncursor:|\n\nmap:|$))", observation_text)
    if message_match:
        message_content = message_match.group(1).strip()
        
        inventory_items_found_list = []
        
        # Regex to find all inventory items. Captures the key and the description.
        # It looks for a letter, a hyphen, then captures until a literal dot followed by a space and another letter-hyphen pattern, or end of string.
        # This will need to be robust for the examples
        # Let's try iterating through lines as it's more robust to inline multiple items:
        
        remaining_messages = message_content
        
        lines = message_content.split('\n')
        # Using a list to hold temporary message contents that are not items
        non_item_lines = []

        for line in lines:
            # Match for an inventory item pattern: single lowercase letter, a hyphen, then the item description
            item_match = re.match(r"^\s*([a-z])\s*-\s*(.+)$", line)
            if item_match:
                inventory_items_found_list.append(f"Inventory item '{item_match.group(1)}': {item_match.group(2).strip()}.")
            elif line.strip(): # If it's not an item and not just whitespace, add to non_item_lines
                non_item_lines.append(line.strip())

        extracted_info.extend(inventory_items_found_list)

        # Reconstruct the remaining message content from non_item_lines
        final_game_message = "\n".join(non_item_lines).strip()
        if final_game_message and not re.match(r"^\s*$", final_game_message):
            extracted_info.append(f"Game_message: {final_game_message}.")

    # Extract agent stats
    stats_match = re.search(
        r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",
        observation_text
    )
    if stats_match:
        dlvl, gold, current_hp, max_hp, current_pw, max_pw, ac, current_xp, next_level_xp = stats_match.groups()
        extracted_info.append(
            f"Agent_stats: Dlvl:{dlvl}, Gold:{gold}, HP:{current_hp}/{max_hp}, Pw:{current_pw}/{max_pw}, AC:{ac}, Xp:{current_xp}/{next_level_xp}."
        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)

```python
        map_section_raw = ""
        if map_end_offset != -1:
            map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag[:map_end_offset].strip('\n')
        else:
            # If no clear end marker, take the rest of the map_content_after_tag
            # This accounts for cases where the map might be followed immediately by E.g. "You can't do that." or similar short messages, then end of string.
            # However, the previous general message parsing also handles this.
            # To be safe, ensure this doesn't consume valid messages after the map.
            # Given the `message:` section exists before `map:`, this branch likely won't be hit for messages.
            # It should ideally just take the remaining map text if any.
            map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag.strip('\n')


        if map_section_raw:
            map_lines = map_section_raw.split('\n')
            
            # Find the agent symbol '@' on the parsed map_lines (which now include original padding)
            visual_agent_x_on_map, visual_agent_y_on_map = -1, -1
            for r_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):

                            molten_lava.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                        elif char == 'F': 
                            fountains.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                        elif char == '<': 
                            stairs_up.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                        # Add more symbols and their interpretations as needed

                if walls:
                    extracted_info.append(f"Walls observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(walls)))}.")
                if open_floor:
                    extracted_info.append(f"Open floor observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(open_floor)))}.")
                if molten_lava:

        lines = message_content.split('\n')

        # It finds a letter key, then collects the description. It uses a lookahead to find either
        # another item key or the end of the line/string.
        combined_item_line_pattern = re.compile(r"([a-z]\s*-\s*[^.]+?(?:\.|\s*(?=[a-z]\s*-|$)))")
        
        for line in lines:
            if not line.strip(): # Skip empty lines
                continue

            found_items_on_line = False
            # Try to find multiple items on a single line
            for item_part_match in combined_item_line_pattern.finditer(line):
                # Now parse each item part individually
                single_item_match = re.match(r"^\s*([a-z])\s*-\s*(.+?)\s*(\.|\s*)$", item_part_match.group(0))
                if single_item_match:
                    inventory_items_found_list.append(f"Inventory item '{single_item_match.group(1)}': {single_item_match.group(2).strip()}.")
                    found_items_on_line = True
            
            # If no items were found on the line, or remaining parts of the line are not items
            if not found_items_on_line and line.strip(): # Ensure it's not just whitespace
                non_item_lines.append(line.strip())

        extracted_info.extend(inventory_items_found_list)

        # Reconstruct the remaining message content from non_item_lines
        final_game_message = "\n".join(non_item_lines).strip()
        if final_game_message and not re.match(r"^\s*$", final_game_message):
            extracted_info.append(f"Game_message: {final_game_message}.")

    # Extract agent stats
    stats_match = re.search(
        r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",
        observation_text
    )
    if stats_match:
        dlvl, gold, current_hp, max_hp, current_pw, max_pw, ac, current_xp, next_level_xp = stats_match.groups()
        extracted_info.append(
            f"Agent_stats: Dlvl:{dlvl}, Gold:{gold}, HP:{current_hp}/{max_hp}, Pw:{current_pw}/{max_pw}, AC:{ac}, Xp:{current_xp}/{next_level_xp}."
        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)
```

```python
                        visual_agent_y_on_map = r_idx

                if visual_agent_x_on_map != -1 and visual_agent_y_on_map != -1:
                    offset_x = agent_x - visual_agent_x_on_map

                    walls = []

                            elif char == '-':

                            elif char == '.':

                            elif char == '}':

                    if walls:

                    if open_floor:

                    if molten_lava:

                    if stairs_up:

    # Extract inventory items and general game messages
    message_content = ""

    # Let's simplify and make explicit: one item at a time.

    if cleaned_general_message:

        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)
```

The core issue is that the death message does not contain item patterns. `item_pattern.finditer(message_content)` should ideally return no matches for the death message.

Let's look at `item_pattern = re.compile(r"(?<!\S)([a-z]) - ([^.\n]+?)(?:\.(?=\s*[a-z] -)|\.$|\n)", re.DOTALL)` on:
`0 Agent-Arc-Dwa-Mal-Law died in The Dungeons of Doom on`
The regex `([a-z]) -` would look for `t - ` in 'Agent-Arc-Dwa-Mal-Law died'. It should *not* match because 't' is not preceded by a non-whitespace boundary or start of line. This is the purpose of `(?<!\S)`.
However, the `(?<!\S)` might prevent initial items like `j - a horn.` at the start of the `message:` block if `message_content` doesn't start with a newline there and it's not a start of string for regex. The problem shows up with `Inventory item 't': Arc-Dwa-Mal-Law died in The Dungeons of Doom on.`. This implies `t` was matched, but it shouldn't have been if `(?<!\S)` worked, or if `message_content` had extra leading spaces.
Let's carefully parse the message content.

The `message_content` is first extracted. It's often multi-line.
`message_match = re.search(r"message:\n([\s\S]+?)(?=\n\ncursor:|\n\nmap:|$)", observation_text)`
`message_content = message_match.group(1).strip()` this strips leading/trailing newlines and spaces. The death message starts with a newline.
`message:`
` No Points Name Hp [max]`
...
The `strip()` call will remove the leading newline, so the " No..." line is at the actual start of `message_content`. Then `(?<!\S)` would make sure `([a-z])` is not preceded by a non-whitespace character, which is exactly what we want.
In `0 Agent-Arc-Dwa-Mal-Law died`, the 't' in 'Agent' is preceded by 'n'. This should NOT match `(?<!\S)t`. So the problem must be elsewhere.

The previous parse output:
`Inventory item 't': Arc-Dwa-Mal-Law died in The Dungeons of Doom on.`
`Inventory item 'c': Dw.`
This indicates that the regex successfully matched `t -` and `c -` which are not actual inventory items. The problem is that the `([^.\n]+?)` part captures `Arc-Dwa-Mal-Law died in The Dungeons of Doom on` for 't', and 'Dw' for 'c'. The pattern `([a-z]) -` is still too broad. `t` and `c` are occurring in the text, not as explicit inventory keys.

Let's simplify the inventory item parsing again. If the intention is that inventory items are always of the form `[letter] - [description].` on their own logical line:

```python
def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:

                            if char == '|':

                            elif char == '.':

                            elif char == '}':
                                molten_lava.append((abs_x, abs_y))

                                fountains.append((abs_x, abs_y))

                                stairs_up.append((abs_x, abs_y))

                    if walls:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Walls observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(walls)))}.")
                    if open_floor:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Open floor observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(open_floor)))}.")

                        extracted_info.append(f"Molten lava (lethal, impassable) observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(molten_lava)))}.")
                    if fountains:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Fountains observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(fountains)))}.")
                    if stairs_up:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Stairs up observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_up)))}.")

    # Extract inventory items and general game messages
    message_content_raw = ""
    message_match = re.search(r"message:\n([\s\S]+?)(?=\n\ncursor:|\n\nmap:|$)", observation_text)
    if message_match:
        message_content_raw = message_match.group(1) # Keep raw to handle newlines and spaces better for item matching

    inventory_items_found = []
    general_messages = []
    
    # Process message content line by line to identify items more accurately
    lines = message_content_raw.strip().split('\n')
    temp_lines = []
    
    item_line_detected = False
    
    # Pattern for an inventory item line: starts with a letter, then " - ", then description, ending with a period.
    item_regex = re.compile(r"^\s*([a-z]) - (.*?)\.$")


    remaining_lines_for_general_message = []
    for line in lines:
        item_match = item_regex.match(line)
        if item_match:
            # Check if this could be part of a multi-item line, like "item1.  item2."
            # Simpler approach: if a line contains "X - item_desc." use a regex that captures everything until the next "LETTER - " or end of line.
            # This is tricky because the sample "j - a horn.  k - a pine wand." is a single line, but two items.
            # Let's handle the multi-item line directly.
            
            # Resetting approach for single-line multi-item parsing
            # Search for all items from the original message_content_raw, then remove them.
            pass
        else:
            remaining_lines_for_general_message.append(line)

    # Re-approach: first, extract all item strings using a flexible regex to handle multi-item lines.
    # The item string could be "j - a horn." or "j - a horn.  k - a pine wand." (if on the same line)
    # The previous regex (r"([a-z]) - (.*?)(?:\.  (?=[a-z] -)|\.$|\n)") was actually decent, but it needs to be applied carefully.
    
    # Let's refine the one that failed the "Agent died" case
    # A true inventory item starts with (a letter) - (description of item).  Must ensure it's not a false positive
    # like 't' from 'Agent-Arc'. It must be at the very start of line or after full stop.
    
    # Try with a modified item regex in the full message block.
    # It must start on a new line or directly after a period and two spaces, to distinguish from arbitrary text.
    
    # Resetting the message extraction logic.
    inventory_items = []
    temp_message_content = message_content_raw.strip()

    # Pattern for individual inventory items.
    # It accounts for items possibly appearing on the same line, separated by '.  ', or ending a line.
    # It *must* start with a letter and a dash. The `(?:^\s*|\.  )` ensures it's either at the beginning of a line (after optional whitespace)
    # or after a preceding item.
    item_pattern_refined = re.compile(r"(?:^\s*|\.  )([a-z]) - ([^.\n]+?\.)", re.MULTILINE)

    # Use finditer to get all matches. The regex is designed to only capture `[a-z] - item_desc.`
    # For "j - a horn.  k - a pine wand."
    # First match: `j - a horn.` (group 1: 'j', group 2: 'a horn.')
    # Second match: `k - a pine wand.` (group 1: 'k', group 2: 'a pine wand.')
    # This should yield correct separate items.
    
    # Keep track of the parts of the message that are items, and remove them later.
    item_spans = []
    for match in item_pattern_refined.finditer(temp_message_content):
        item_key = match.group(1).strip()
        item_description = match.group(2).strip()
        inventory_items.append(f"Inventory item '{item_key}': {item_description}.")
        item_spans.append(match.span()) # Store the start and end indices of the match

    # Reconstruct the general message by removing the item spans
    # Sort spans in reverse to avoid index shifting issues during removal
    item_spans.sort(key=lambda x: x[0], reverse=True)
    
    # Convert string to list of characters for easier modification
    message_chars = list(temp_message_content)
    
    for start, end in item_spans:
        # Replace the matched item text with spaces so that other parts of the message are preserved
        # This will remove exact matches of "j - a horn." or "k - a pine wand."
        for i in range(start, end):
            message_chars[i] = ' ' # Replace with space or empty string

    remaining_message = "".join(message_chars)
    
    # Clean up excess whitespace and newline characters
    cleaned_general_message_lines = [line.strip() for line in remaining_message.strip().split('\n') if line.strip()]
    cleaned_general_message = '\n'.join(cleaned_general_message_lines)

    # Add inventory items
    if inventory_items:
        extracted_info.extend(inventory_items)

    # Add general messages, excluding cursor lines or purely whitespace/punctuation messages
    if cleaned_general_message and not cleaned_general_message.startswith("cursor:"):
        # Further filter out messages like "..." or empty strings after stripping
        if len(cleaned_general_message) > 3 or not re.match(r"^[. ]+$", cleaned_general_message):
            if "St:15 Dx:9 Co:14 In:14 Wi:14 Ch:9 Lawful S:0" not in cleaned_general_message: # Avoid including stat line if it gets appended here
                general_messages.append(f"Game_message: {cleaned_general_message}.")

    if general_messages:
        extracted_info.extend(general_messages)

    # Extract agent stats, ensuring not to conflict with game message
    stats_match = re.search(
        r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",
        observation_text
    )
    if stats_match:
        dlvl, gold, current_hp, max_hp, current_pw, max_pw, ac, current_xp, next_level_xp = stats_match.groups()
        extracted_info.append(
            f"Agent_stats: Dlvl:{dlvl}, Gold:{gold}, HP:{current_hp}/{max_hp}, Pw:{current_pw}/{max_pw}, AC:{ac}, Xp:{current_xp}/{next_level_xp}."
        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)
```

The line-by-line processing of items seems like a more robust way to differentiate.
`individual_item_pattern = re.compile(r"([a-z]) - ([^.\n]+?\.)")`
`j - a horn. k - a pine wand.`
- Match 1: group1='j', group2='a horn.'. `inventory_items` gets `Inventory item 'j': a horn.`
- Match 2: group1='k', group2='a pine wand.'. `inventory_items` gets `Inventory item 'k': a pine wand.`

```python
import re

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    extracted_info = []

    # Extract agent's absolute position
    agent_x, agent_y = None, None
    agent_pos_match = re.search(r"cursor:\s*(?:Yourself a archeologist)?\s*\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    if agent_pos_match:
        agent_x = int(agent_pos_match.group(1))
        agent_y = int(agent_pos_match.group(2))
        extracted_info.append(f"Agent_absolute_position: ({agent_x}, {agent_y}).")

    # Extract map information if agent position is known
    if agent_x is not None and agent_y is not None:
        map_start_idx = observation_text.find("map:\n")
        if map_start_idx != -1:
            map_content_after_tag = observation_text[map_start_idx + len("map:\n"):]

            map_end_marker_1 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nAgent the Digger")
            map_end_marker_2 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nSt:")
            
            map_end_offset = -1
            if map_end_marker_1 != -1 and (map_end_marker_2 == -1 or map_end_marker_1 < map_end_marker_2):
                map_end_offset = map_end_marker_1
            elif map_end_marker_2 != -1:
                map_end_offset = map_end_marker_2
            
            map_section_raw = ""
            if map_end_offset != -1:
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag[:map_end_offset].strip('\n')
            else:
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag.strip('\n') 

            if map_section_raw:
                map_lines = map_section_raw.split('\n')
                
                visual_agent_x_on_map, visual_agent_y_on_map = -1, -1
                for r_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                    if '@' in line:
                        visual_agent_x_on_map = line.find('@')
                        visual_agent_y_on_map = r_idx
                        break
                
                if visual_agent_x_on_map != -1 and visual_agent_y_on_map != -1:
                    offset_x = agent_x - visual_agent_x_on_map
                    offset_y = agent_y - visual_agent_y_on_map

                    walls = []
                    open_floor = []
                    molten_lava = []
                    fountains = []
                    stairs_up = []
                    
                    for y_offset, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                        for x_offset, char in enumerate(line):
                            abs_x = x_offset + offset_x
                            abs_y = y_offset + offset_y

                            if char == '|':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'vertical'))
                            elif char == '-':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'horizontal'))
                            elif char == '.':
                                open_floor.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '}':
                                molten_lava.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == 'F':
                                fountains.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '<':
                                stairs_up.append((abs_x, abs_y))

                    if walls:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Walls observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(walls)))}.")
                    if open_floor:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Open floor observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(open_floor)))}.")
                    if molten_lava:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Molten lava (lethal, impassable) observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(molten_lava)))}.")
                    if fountains:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Fountains observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(fountains)))}.")
                    if stairs_up:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Stairs up observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_up)))}.")

    # Extract inventory items and general game messages
    message_content_raw = ""
    message_match = re.search(r"message:\n([\s\S]+?)(?=\n\ncursor:|\n\nmap:|$)", observation_text)
    if message_match:
        message_content_raw = message_match.group(1)

    inventory_items = []
    general_messages_content = []
    
    # Pattern to find individual inventory items on a line or within a line, separated by '.  '
    # Captures: 1: item_key (char), 2: item_description (text without trailing dot)
    # The lookahead `(?=\.  ?[a-z] -|\.)` ensures the description ends before a dot which is followed by
    # another potential item or just ends the full item description. 
    # Use re.findall to get all occurrences, then we can remove them.
    # The output format for items is 'j': a horn. (without trailing period).
    
    # Let's try to match full inventory lines (multiple items per line allowed)
    initial_message_lines = message_content_raw.strip().split('\n')
    
    item_pattern_on_line = re.compile(r"([a-z]) - ([^.]+?)(?=\.  |[.])") # Match key and desc up to a period or '  '
    
    for line in initial_message_lines:
        line_contains_pure_items = False
        potential_items_in_line = []
        for match in item_pattern_on_line.finditer(line):
            item_key = match.group(1)
            item_description = match.group(2).strip()
            potential_items_in_line.append(f"Inventory item '{item_key}': {item_description}.")
            
            # Check if the entire line possibly consists solely of items (like "j - a. k - b.")
            # This is heuristic, but helps distinguish item lists from arbitrary text
            # A simple way to check: if the first non-space character is `[a-z] - ` and it ends with a period,
            # it's likely an item line. This will be done below to filter general messages.

        if potential_items_in_line:
            # If any items were found on the line, assume it's an item-listing line
            # and consume it into inventory_items. This heuristic means that a line
            # might have some other text but if it has `x - item.`, we extract it.
            # This is based on the assumption that inventory is always cleanly presented.
            inventory_items.extend(potential_items_in_line)
        else:
            # If no items explicitly matched, it's a general message line.
            general_messages_content.append(line)

    cleaned_general_message = '\n'.join([l.strip() for l in general_messages_content if l.strip()])

    if inventory_items:
        extracted_info.extend(inventory_items)

    if cleaned_general_message and not cleaned_general_message.startswith("cursor:"): # Filter out cursor info
        if len(cleaned_general_message) > 3 or not re.match(r"^[. ]+$", cleaned_general_message): # Filter short/empty messages
            # Filter out known stat lines that occasionally appear in the message section
            if not re.search(r"Dlvl:\d+.*Xp:\d+/\d+", cleaned_general_message): 
                extracted_info.append(f"Game_message: {cleaned_general_message}.")

    # Extract agent stats
    stats_match = re.search(
        r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",
        observation_text
    )
    if stats_match:
        dlvl, gold, current_hp, max_hp, current_pw, max_pw, ac, current_xp, next_level_xp = stats_match.groups()
        extracted_info.append(
            f"Agent_stats: Dlvl:{dlvl}, Gold:{gold}, HP:{current_hp}/{max_hp}, Pw:{current_pw}/{max_pw}, AC:{ac}, Xp:{current_xp}/{next_level_xp}."
        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)


```

Let's test the `item_pattern_on_line` regex: `r"([a-z]) - ([^.]+?)(?=\.  |[.])"` on `j - a horn. k - a pine wand.`
1. First `match`: `group 1='j'`, `group 2='a horn'`. The `(?=\.  |[\.])` matches the `.` after `horn`. Output: "Inventory item 'j': a horn."
2. Second `match`: `group 1='k'`, `group 2='a pine wand'`. The `(?=\.  |[\.])` matches the `.` after `wand`. Output: "Inventory item 'k': a pine wand."
This correctly identifies both items and provides the description without the trailing period. This looks good.

For the empty message in step 4: `message: \n\ncursor:...`:
`message_content_raw` will receive an empty string from `message_match.group(1)`.
`initial_message_lines` will be `[]` after `.strip().split('\n')`.
The loop `for line in initial_message_lines:` won't execute.
`cleaned_general_message` will be `""`.
So `if cleaned_general_message` will be false, preventing the `Game_message` output. This looks correct.

For the death message in step 9:
`message_content_raw` will contain the multi-line death message.
`item_pattern_on_line` will not find `([a-z]) - ` in the death message lines.
`potential_items_in_line` will be empty for all those lines.
So these lines will correctly be added to `general_messages_content`.
`cleaned_general_message` will be the entire death message, which will be outputted as a `Game_message`.
This also seems correct.

```python
            # If no inventory items were found on this specific line, consider it part of the general message.
            general_messages_content.append(line)

        # Filter out messages that are too short/just punctuation, or contain stat line info (which are extracted separately)
        if (len(cleaned_general_message) > 3 or not re.match(r"^[. ]+$", cleaned_general_message)) and \
           not re.search(r"Dlvl:\d+.*Xp:\d+/\d+", cleaned_general_message) and \
           not re.search(r"St:\d+ Dx:\d+ Co:\d+ In:\d+ Wi:\d+ Ch:\d+ Lawful S:\d+", cleaned_general_message):
            extracted_info.append(f"Game_message: {cleaned_general_message}.")

    # Extract agent stats
    stats_match = re.search(
        r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)
```

feedback_history.qa_forward_prompt:
You are an agent that has been interacting with an environment. Based on your current knowledge, answer each question below with YES or NO.

=== YOUR KNOWLEDGE ===
<world_knowledge>
- Moving "north" changes the agent's y-coordinate by -1.
- Moving "east" changes the agent's x-coordinate by +1.
- The '@' symbol represents the agent's current position.
- The '<' symbol represents the stairs leading up to the previous level.
- The `---` and `|` symbols represent solid walls that cannot be opened.
- The `}` symbol represents molten lava, a lethal and impassable environmental feature.
- Attempting to `open` a `}` symbol results in "You see no door there."
- Attempting to `kick` a `}` symbol results in "Dumb move! You strain a muscle."
- Attempting to move onto a `}` symbol is not possible; the agent remains in its current position.
- Both `open` and `kick` actions consume a turn and require a direction argument.

The agent receives the following default instructions/knowledge:
=== DEFAULT KNOWLEDGE ===
You are an agent interacting with an environment. 
Your goal is to learn as much about the environment as possible
The following are the possible actions you can take, followed by a short description of each action -

pickup: pick up things at the current location if there are any,
pray: pray to the gods for help,
puton: put on an accessory,
quaff: quaff (drink) something,
search: search for hidden doors and passages,
zap: zap a wand.
</actions>
=== END DEFAULT KNOWLEDGE ===

=== CURRENT BELIEFS ===
<world_knowledge>
- Moving "north" changes the agent's y-coordinate by -1.
- Moving "east" changes the agent's x-coordinate by +1.
- The '@' symbol represents the agent's current position.
- The '<' symbol represents the stairs leading up to the previous level.
- The `---` and `|` symbols represent solid walls that cannot be opened.
- The `}` symbol represents molten lava, a lethal and impassable environmental feature.
- Attempting to `open` a `}` symbol results in "You see no door there."
- Attempting to `kick` a `}` symbol results in "Dumb move! You strain a muscle."
- Attempting to move onto a `}` symbol is not possible; the agent remains in its current position.
- Both `open` and `kick` actions consume a turn and require a direction argument.
</world_knowledge>
<policy>
- Prioritize exploring new areas to learn about the environment, but avoid moving onto unknown or unidentifiable terrain features.
- When an interactive object is explicitly identified by the perception module, move towards its *identified coordinates*.
- Disregard the perception module's classification of objects as "closed doors" if the 'open' or 'kick' action fails to interact with them meaningfully.
- If 'open' and 'kick' actions fail on a perception-identified "door", and the feature is identified as 'molten lava' or 'impassable' by the perception module, infer that it is an impassable terrain feature and do not attempt to move onto it.
- Use the `search` action as a preliminary step to reveal hidden doors or passages when starting in a new location or when blocked by seemingly impassable features.
</policy>
=== END CURRENT BELIEFS ===

=== CURRENT PERCEPTION MODULE ===
import re

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    extracted_info = []

    # Extract agent's absolute position
    agent_x, agent_y = None, None
    # Use re.DOTALL to allow '.' to match newlines, and \s* for flexible whitespace matching
    agent_pos_match = re.search(r"cursor:\s*(?:Yourself a archeologist)?\s*\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    if agent_pos_match:
        agent_x = int(agent_pos_match.group(1))
        agent_y = int(agent_pos_match.group(2))
        extracted_info.append(f"Agent_absolute_position: ({agent_x}, {agent_y}).")

    # Extract map information if agent position is known
    if agent_x is not None and agent_y is not None:
        map_start_idx = observation_text.find("map:\n")
        if map_start_idx != -1: # Ensure map section exists
            map_content_after_tag = observation_text[map_start_idx + len("map:\n"):]

            # Define markers for the end of the map content
            map_end_marker_1 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nAgent the Digger")
            map_end_marker_2 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nSt:") # Fallback for stat line

            map_end_offset = -1
            if map_end_marker_1 != -1 and (map_end_marker_2 == -1 or map_end_marker_1 < map_end_marker_2):
                map_end_offset = map_end_marker_1
            elif map_end_marker_2 != -1:
                map_end_offset = map_end_marker_2
            
            map_section_raw = ""
            if map_end_offset != -1:
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag[:map_end_offset].strip('\n')
            else:
                # If no clear end marker like "Agent the Digger" or "St:",
                # take the rest of the content after "map:", assuming it's all map and stripping newlines.
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag.strip('\n')

            if map_section_raw:
                map_lines = map_section_raw.split('\n')
                
                # Find the agent symbol '@' on the parsed map_lines (which now include original padding)
                visual_agent_x_on_map, visual_agent_y_on_map = -1, -1
                for r_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                    if '@' in line:
                        visual_agent_x_on_map = line.find('@')
                        visual_agent_y_on_map = r_idx
                        break
                
                if visual_agent_x_on_map != -1 and visual_agent_y_on_map != -1:
                    # Calculate the offset between visual coordinates and absolute coordinates
                    offset_x = agent_x - visual_agent_x_on_map
                    offset_y = agent_y - visual_agent_y_on_map

                    walls = []
                    open_floor = []
                    molten_lava = []
                    fountains = []
                    stairs_up = []
                    
                    for y_offset, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                        for x_offset, char in enumerate(line):
                            abs_x = x_offset + offset_x
                            abs_y = y_offset + offset_y

                            if char == '|':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'vertical'))
                            elif char == '-':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'horizontal'))
                            elif char == '.':
                                open_floor.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '}': # Classified as molten lava based on world knowledge and policy
                                molten_lava.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == 'F': # Explicitly identify 'F' as fountains
                                fountains.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '<': # Explicitly identify '<' as stairs up
                                stairs_up.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            # Add more symbols and their interpretations as needed

                    if walls:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Walls observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(walls)))}.")
                    if open_floor:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Open floor observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(open_floor)))}.")
                    if molten_lava: # Report molten lava
                        extracted_info.append(f"Molten lava (lethal, impassable) observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(molten_lava)))}.")
                    if fountains:    # Report fountains
                        extracted_info.append(f"Fountains observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(fountains)))}.")
                    if stairs_up:    # Report stairs up
                        extracted_info.append(f"Stairs up observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_up)))}.")

    # Extract inventory items and general game messages
    message_content_raw = ""
    message_match = re.search(r"message:\n([\s\S]+?)(?=\n\ncursor:|\n\nmap:|$)", observation_text)
    if message_match:
        message_content_raw = message_match.group(1)
        
    inventory_items = []
    general_messages_content = []
    
    # Process message content line by line to differentiate items from general messages
    initial_message_lines = message_content_raw.strip().split('\n')
    
    # Pattern to find individual inventory items on a line or within a line, separated by '.  '
    # Captures: 1: item_key (char), 2: item_description (text without trailing dot)
    # This regex is designed to extract a key and its description, stopping before the next period that
    # either marks the end of the item or precedes another item on the same line (separated by '  ').
    item_pattern_on_line = re.compile(r"([a-z]) - ([^.]+?)(?=\.  |\.($|\s*\n))") # The `($|\s*\n)` handles end of content or end of line.

    for line in initial_message_lines:
        line_contains_items = False
        potential_items_in_line = []
        
        # Find all individual item matches within the current line
        for match in item_pattern_on_line.finditer(line):
            item_key = match.group(1)
            item_description = match.group(2).strip()
            potential_items_in_line.append(f"Inventory item '{item_key}': {item_description}.")
            line_contains_items = True 
        
        if line_contains_items:
            # If any items were found on the line, append them to the inventory list.
            inventory_items.extend(potential_items_in_line)
        else:
            # If no inventory items were found on this specific line, consider it part of the general message.
            general_messages_content.append(line)

    cleaned_general_message = '\n'.join([l.strip() for l in general_messages_content if l.strip()])

    if inventory_items:
        extracted_info.extend(inventory_items)

    if cleaned_general_message and not cleaned_general_message.startswith("cursor:"): # Filter out cursor info which is extracted separately
        # Filter out messages that are too short/just punctuation, or contain stat line info (which are extracted separately)
        if (len(cleaned_general_message) > 3 or not re.match(r"^[. ]+$", cleaned_general_message)) and \
           not re.search(r"Dlvl:\d+.*Xp:\d+/\d+", cleaned_general_message) and \
           not re.search(r"St:\d+ Dx:\d+ Co:\d+ In:\d+ Wi:\d+ Ch:\d+ Lawful S:\d+", cleaned_general_message):
            extracted_info.append(f"Game_message: {cleaned_general_message}.")

    # Extract agent stats
    stats_match = re.search(
        r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",
        observation_text
    )
    if stats_match:
        dlvl, gold, current_hp, max_hp, current_pw, max_pw, ac, current_xp, next_level_xp = stats_match.groups()
        extracted_info.append(
            f"Agent_stats: Dlvl:{dlvl}, Gold:{gold}, HP:{current_hp}/{max_hp}, Pw:{current_pw}/{max_pw}, AC:{ac}, Xp:{current_xp}/{next_level_xp}."
        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)
=== END CURRENT PERCEPTION MODULE ===

```python
import re

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    extracted_info = []

    # Extract agent's absolute position
    agent_x, agent_y = None, None
    # Use re.DOTALL to allow '.' to match newlines, and \s* for flexible whitespace matching
    agent_pos_match = re.search(r"cursor:\s*(?:Yourself a archeologist)?\s*\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    if agent_pos_match:
        agent_x = int(agent_pos_match.group(1))
        agent_y = int(agent_pos_match.group(2))
        extracted_info.append(f"Agent_absolute_position: ({agent_x}, {agent_y}).")

    # Extract map information if agent position is known
    if agent_x is not None and agent_y is not None:
        map_start_idx = observation_text.find("map:\n")
        if map_start_idx != -1: # Ensure map section exists
            map_content_after_tag = observation_text[map_start_idx + len("map:\n"):]

            # Define markers for the end of the map content
            map_end_marker_1 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nAgent the Digger")
            map_end_marker_2 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nSt:") # Fallback for stat line

            map_end_offset = -1
            if map_end_marker_1 != -1 and (map_end_marker_2 == -1 or map_end_marker_1 < map_end_marker_2):
                map_end_offset = map_end_marker_1
            elif map_end_marker_2 != -1:
                map_end_offset = map_end_marker_2
            
            map_section_raw = ""
            if map_end_offset != -1:
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag[:map_end_offset].strip('\n')
            else:
                # If no clear end marker like "Agent the Digger" or "St:",
                # take the rest of the content after "map:", assuming it's all map and stripping newlines.
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag.strip('\n')

            if map_section_raw:
                map_lines = map_section_raw.split('\n')
                
                # Find the agent symbol '@' on the parsed map_lines (which now include original padding)
                visual_agent_x_on_map, visual_agent_y_on_map = -1, -1
                for r_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                    if '@' in line:
                        visual_agent_x_on_map = line.find('@')
                        visual_agent_y_on_map = r_idx
                        break
                
                if visual_agent_x_on_map != -1 and visual_agent_y_on_map != -1:
                    # Calculate the offset between visual coordinates and absolute coordinates
                    offset_x = agent_x - visual_agent_x_on_map
                    offset_y = agent_y - visual_agent_y_on_map

                    walls = []
                    open_floor = []
                    molten_lava = []
                    fountains = []
                    stairs_up = []
                    
                    for y_offset, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                        for x_offset, char in enumerate(line):
                            abs_x = x_offset + offset_x
                            abs_y = y_offset + offset_y

                            if char == '|':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'vertical'))
                            elif char == '-':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'horizontal'))
                            elif char == '.':
                                open_floor.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '}': # Classified as molten lava based on world knowledge and policy
                                molten_lava.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == 'F': # Explicitly identify 'F' as fountains
                                fountains.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '<': # Explicitly identify '<' as stairs up
                                stairs_up.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            # Add more symbols and their interpretations as needed

                    if walls:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Walls observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(walls)))}.")
                    if open_floor:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Open floor observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(open_floor)))}.")
                    if molten_lava: # Report molten lava
                        extracted_info.append(f"Molten lava (lethal, impassable) observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(molten_lava)))}.")
                    if fountains:    # Report fountains
                        extracted_info.append(f"Fountains observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(fountains)))}.")
                    if stairs_up:    # Report stairs up
                        extracted_info.append(f"Stairs up observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_up)))}.")

    # Extract inventory items and general game messages
    message_content_raw = ""
    message_match = re.search(r"message:\n([\s\S]+?)(?=\n\ncursor:|\n\nmap:|$)", observation_text)
    if message_match:
        message_content_raw = message_match.group(1)
        
    inventory_items = []
    general_messages_content = []
    
    # Process message content line by line to differentiate items from general messages
    initial_message_lines = message_content_raw.strip().split('\n')
    
    # Pattern to find individual inventory items on a line or within a line, separated by '.  '
    # Captures: 1: item_key (char), 2: item_description (text without trailing dot)
    # This regex is designed to extract a key and its description, stopping before the next period that
    # either marks the end of the item or precedes another item on the same line (separated by '  ').
    item_pattern_on_line = re.compile(r"([a-z]) - ([^.]+?)(?=\.  |\.($|\s*\n))") # The `($|\s*\n)` handles end of content or end of line.

    for line in initial_message_lines:
        line_contains_items = False
```

```python
import re

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    extracted_info = []

    # Extract agent's absolute position
    agent_x, agent_y = None, None
    # Use re.DOTALL to allow '.' to match newlines, and \s* for flexible whitespace matching
    agent_pos_match = re.search(r"cursor:\s*(?:Yourself a archeologist|Yourself a priestess)?\s*\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    if agent_pos_match:
        agent_x = int(agent_pos_match.group(1))
        agent_y = int(agent_pos_match.group(2))
        extracted_info.append(f"Agent_absolute_position: ({agent_x}, {agent_y}).")

    # Extract map information if agent position is known
    if agent_x is not None and agent_y is not None:
        map_start_idx = observation_text.find("map:\n")
        if map_start_idx != -1: # Ensure map section exists
            map_content_after_tag = observation_text[map_start_idx + len("map:\n"):]

            # Define markers for the end of the map content
            map_end_marker_1 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nAgent the Digger")
            map_end_marker_2 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nAgent the Aspirant") # New agent type
            map_end_marker_3 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nSt:") # Fallback for stat line

            map_end_offset = -1
            if map_end_marker_1 != -1 and (map_end_marker_2 == -1 or map_end_marker_1 < map_end_marker_2) and (map_end_marker_3 == -1 or map_end_marker_1 < map_end_marker_3):
                map_end_offset = map_end_marker_1
            elif map_end_marker_2 != -1 and (map_end_marker_1 == -1 or map_end_marker_2 < map_end_marker_1) and (map_end_marker_3 == -1 or map_end_marker_2 < map_end_marker_3):
                map_end_offset = map_end_marker_2
            elif map_end_marker_3 != -1:
                map_end_offset = map_end_marker_3
            
            map_section_raw = ""
            if map_end_offset != -1:
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag[:map_end_offset].strip('\n')
            else:
                # If no clear end marker, take the rest of the content after "map:", assuming it's all map.
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag.strip('\n')

            if map_section_raw:
                map_lines = map_section_raw.split('\n')
                
                # Find the agent symbol '@' on the parsed map_lines
                visual_agent_x_on_map, visual_agent_y_on_map = -1, -1
                for r_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                    if '@' in line:
                        visual_agent_x_on_map = line.find('@')
                        visual_agent_y_on_map = r_idx
                        break
                
                if visual_agent_x_on_map != -1 and visual_agent_y_on_map != -1:
                    # Calculate the offset between visual coordinates and absolute coordinates
                    offset_x = agent_x - visual_agent_x_on_map
                    offset_y = agent_y - visual_agent_y_on_map

                    walls = []
                    open_floor = []
                    molten_lava = []
                    fountains = []
                    stairs_up = []
                    
                    for y_offset, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                        for x_offset, char in enumerate(line):
                            abs_x = x_offset + offset_x
                            abs_y = y_offset + offset_y

                            if char == '|':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'vertical'))
                            elif char == '-':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'horizontal'))
                            elif char == '.':
                                open_floor.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '}': # Classified as molten lava
                                molten_lava.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == 'F': # Explicitly identify 'F' as fountains
                                fountains.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '<': # Explicitly identify '<' as stairs up
                                stairs_up.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            # Add more symbols and their interpretations as needed

                    if walls:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Walls observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(walls)))}.")
                    if open_floor:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Open floor observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(open_floor)))}.")
                    if molten_lava: # Report molten lava
                        extracted_info.append(f"Molten lava (lethal, impassable) observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(molten_lava)))}.")
                    if fountains:    # Report fountains
                        extracted_info.append(f"Fountains observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(fountains)))}.")
                    if stairs_up:    # Report stairs up
                        extracted_info.append(f"Stairs up observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_up)))}.")

    # Extract inventory items and general game messages
    message_content_raw = ""
    message_match = re.search(r"message:\n([\s\S]+?)(?=\n\ncursor:|\n\nmap:|$)", observation_text)
    if message_match:
        message_content_raw = message_match.group(1)
        
    inventory_items = []
    general_messages_content = []
    
    item_pattern_on_line = re.compile(r"([a-z]) - ([^.]+?)(?=\.  |\.($|\s*\n))")

    for line in message_content_raw.strip().split('\n'):
        line_contains_items = False
        potential_items_in_line = []
        
        for match in item_pattern_on_line.finditer(line):
            item_key = match.group(1)
            item_description = match.group(2).strip()
            potential_items_in_line.append(f"Inventory item '{item_key}': {item_description}.")
            line_contains_items = True 
        
        if line_contains_items:
            inventory_items.extend(potential_items_in_line)
        else:
            general_messages_content.append(line)

    cleaned_general_message = '\n'.join([l.strip() for l in general_messages_content if l.strip()])

    if inventory_items:
        extracted_info.extend(inventory_items)

    if cleaned_general_message and not cleaned_general_message.startswith("cursor:"): # Filter out cursor info
        if (len(cleaned_general_message) > 3 or not re.match(r"^[. ]+$", cleaned_general_message)) and \
           not re.search(r"Dlvl:\d+.*Xp:\d+/\d+", cleaned_general_message) and \
           not re.search(r"St:\d+ Dx:\d+ Co:\d+ In:\d+ Wi:\d+ Ch:\d+ Lawful S:\d+", cleaned_general_message):
            extracted_info.append(f"Game_message: {cleaned_general_message}.")

    # Extract agent stats
    # Updated regex to also capture "Agent the Aspirant" for new character type if needed and general stat line
    stats_match = re.search(
        r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",
        observation_text
    )
    if stats_match:
        dlvl, gold, current_hp, max_hp, current_pw, max_pw, ac, current_xp, next_level_xp = stats_match.groups()
        extracted_info.append(
            f"Agent_stats: Dlvl:{dlvl}, Gold:{gold}, HP:{current_hp}/{max_hp}, Pw:{current_pw}/{max_pw}, AC:{ac}, Xp:{current_xp}/{next_level_xp}."
        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)
```

```python
import re

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    extracted_info = []

    # Extract agent's absolute position
    agent_x, agent_y = None, None
    # Use re.DOTALL to allow '.' to match newlines, and \s* for flexible whitespace matching
    agent_pos_match = re.search(r"cursor:\s*(?:Yourself a archeologist)?\s*\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    if agent_pos_match:
        agent_x = int(agent_pos_match.group(1))
        agent_y = int(agent_pos_match.group(2))
        extracted_info.append(f"Agent_absolute_position: ({agent_x}, {agent_y}).")

    # Extract map information if agent position is known
    if agent_x is not None and agent_y is not None:
        map_start_idx = observation_text.find("map:\n")
        if map_start_idx != -1: # Ensure map section exists
            map_content_after_tag = observation_text[map_start_idx + len("map:\n"):]

            # Define markers for the end of the map content
            map_end_marker_1 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nAgent the Digger")
            map_end_marker_2 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nSt:") # Fallback for stat line

            map_end_offset = -1
            if map_end_marker_1 != -1 and (map_end_marker_2 == -1 or map_end_marker_1 < map_end_marker_2):
                map_end_offset = map_end_marker_1
            elif map_end_marker_2 != -1:
                map_end_offset = map_end_marker_2
            
            map_section_raw = ""
            if map_end_offset != -1:
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag[:map_end_offset].strip('\n')
            else:
                # If no clear end marker like "Agent the Digger" or "St:",
                # take the rest of the content after "map:", assuming it's all map and stripping newlines.
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag.strip('\n')

            if map_section_raw:
                map_lines = map_section_raw.split('\n')
                
                # Find the agent symbol '@' on the parsed map_lines (which now include original padding)
                visual_agent_x_on_map, visual_agent_y_on_map = -1, -1
                for r_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                    if '@' in line:
                        visual_agent_x_on_map = line.find('@')
                        visual_agent_y_on_map = r_idx
                        break
                
                if visual_agent_x_on_map != -1 and visual_agent_y_on_map != -1:
                    # Calculate the offset between visual coordinates and absolute coordinates
                    offset_x = agent_x - visual_agent_x_on_map
                    offset_y = agent_y - visual_agent_y_on_map

                    walls = []
                    open_floor = []
                    molten_lava = []
                    fountains = []
                    stairs_up = []
                    
                    for y_offset, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                        for x_offset, char in enumerate(line):
                            abs_x = x_offset + offset_x
                            abs_y = y_offset + offset_y

                            if char == '|':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'vertical'))
                            elif char == '-':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'horizontal'))
                            elif char == '.':
                                open_floor.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '}': # Classified as molten lava based on world knowledge and policy
                                molten_lava.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == 'F': # Explicitly identify 'F' as fountains
                                fountains.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '<': # Explicitly identify '<' as stairs up
                                stairs_up.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            # Add more symbols and their interpretations as needed

                    if walls:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Walls observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(walls)))}.")
                    if open_floor:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Open floor observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(open_floor)))}.")
                    if molten_lava: # Report molten lava
                        extracted_info.append(f"Molten lava (lethal, impassable) observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(molten_lava)))}.")
                    if fountains:    # Report fountains
                        extracted_info.append(f"Fountains observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(fountains)))}.")
                    if stairs_up:    # Report stairs up
                        extracted_info.append(f"Stairs up observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_up)))}.")

    # Extract inventory items and general game messages
    message_content_raw = ""
    message_match = re.search(r"message:\n([\s\S]+?)(?=\n\ncursor:|\n\nmap:|$)", observation_text)
    if message_match:
        message_content_raw = message_match.group(1)
        
    inventory_items = []
    general_messages_content = []
    
    # Process message content line by line to differentiate items from general messages
    initial_message_lines = message_content_raw.strip().split('\n')
    
    # Pattern to find individual inventory items on a line or within a line, separated by '.  '
    # Captures: 1: item_key (char), 2: item_description (text without trailing dot)
    # This regex is designed to extract a key and its description, stopping before the next period that
    # either marks the end of the item or precedes another item on the same line (separated by '  ').
    item_pattern_on_line = re.compile(r"([a-z]) - ([^.]+?)(?=\.  |\.($|\s*\n))") # The `($|\s*\n)` handles end of content or end of line.

    for line in initial_message_lines:
        line_contains_items = False
        potential_items_in_line = [] # Corrected variable name
        
        # Find all individual item matches within the current line
        for match in item_pattern_on_line.finditer(line):
            item_key = match.group(1)
            item_description = match.group(2).strip()
            potential_items_in_line.append(f"Inventory item '{item_key}': {item_description}.")
            line_contains_items = True 
        
        if line_contains_items:
            # If any items were found on the line, append them to the inventory list.
            inventory_items.extend(potential_items_in_line)
        else:
            # If no inventory items were found on this specific line, consider it part of the general message.
            general_messages_content.append(line)

    cleaned_general_message = '\n'.join([l.strip() for l in general_messages_content if l.strip()])

    if inventory_items:
        extracted_info.extend(inventory_items)

    if cleaned_general_message and not cleaned_general_message.startswith("cursor:"): # Filter out cursor info which is extracted separately
        # Filter out messages that are too short/just punctuation, or contain stat line info (which are extracted separately)
        if (len(cleaned_general_message) > 3 or not re.match(r"^[. ]+$", cleaned_general_message)) and \
           not re.search(r"Dlvl:\d+.*Xp:\d+/\d+", cleaned_general_message) and \
           not re.search(r"St:\d+ Dx:\d+ Co:\d+ In:\d+ Wi:\d+ Ch:\d+ Lawful S:\d+", cleaned_general_message):
            extracted_info.append(f"Game_message: {cleaned_general_message}.")

    # Extract agent stats
    stats_match = re.search(
        r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",
        observation_text
    )
    if stats_match:
        dlvl, gold, current_hp, max_hp, current_pw, max_pw, ac, current_xp, next_level_xp = stats_match.groups()
        extracted_info.append(
            f"Agent_stats: Dlvl:{dlvl}, Gold:{gold}, HP:{current_hp}/{max_hp}, Pw:{current_pw}/{max_pw}, AC:{ac}, Xp:{current_xp}/{next_level_xp}."
        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)
```
