```python
import re

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    extracted_info = []

    # Extract agent's absolute position
    agent_x, agent_y = None, None
    # Use re.DOTALL to allow '.' to match newlines, and \s* for flexible whitespace matching
    agent_pos_match = re.search(r"cursor:\s*(?:Yourself a archeologist)?\s*\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    if agent_pos_match:
        agent_x = int(agent_pos_match.group(1))
        agent_y = int(agent_pos_match.group(2))
        extracted_info.append(f"Agent_absolute_position: ({agent_x}, {agent_y}).")

    # Extract map information if agent position is known
    if agent_x is not None and agent_y is not None:
        map_start_idx = observation_text.find("map:\n")
        if map_start_idx != -1: # Ensure map section exists
            map_content_after_tag = observation_text[map_start_idx + len("map:\n"):]

            # Define markers for the end of the map content
            map_end_marker_1 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nAgent the Digger")
            map_end_marker_2 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nSt:") # Fallback for stat line

            map_end_offset = -1
            if map_end_marker_1 != -1 and (map_end_marker_2 == -1 or map_end_marker_1 < map_end_marker_2):
                map_end_offset = map_end_marker_1
            elif map_end_marker_2 != -1:
                map_end_offset = map_end_marker_2
            
            map_section_raw = ""
            if map_end_offset != -1:
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag[:map_end_offset].strip('\n')
            else:
                # If no clear end marker like "Agent the Digger" or "St:",
                # take the rest of the content after "map:", assuming it's all map and stripping newlines.
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag.strip('\n')

            if map_section_raw:
                map_lines = map_section_raw.split('\n')
                
                # Find the agent symbol '@' on the parsed map_lines (which now include original padding)
                visual_agent_x_on_map, visual_agent_y_on_map = -1, -1
                for r_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                    if '@' in line:
                        visual_agent_x_on_map = line.find('@')
                        visual_agent_y_on_map = r_idx
                        break
                
                if visual_agent_x_on_map != -1 and visual_agent_y_on_map != -1:
                    # Calculate the offset between visual coordinates and absolute coordinates
                    offset_x = agent_x - visual_agent_x_on_map
                    offset_y = agent_y - visual_agent_y_on_map

                    walls = []
                    open_floor = []
                    molten_lava = []
                    fountains = []
                    stairs_up = []
                    
                    for y_offset, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                        for x_offset, char in enumerate(line):
                            abs_x = x_offset + offset_x
                            abs_y = y_offset + offset_y

                            if char == '|':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'vertical'))
                            elif char == '-':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'horizontal'))
                            elif char == '.':
                                open_floor.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '}': # Classified as molten lava based on world knowledge and policy
                                molten_lava.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == 'F': # Explicitly identify 'F' as fountains
                                fountains.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '<': # Explicitly identify '<' as stairs up
                                stairs_up.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            # Add more symbols and their interpretations as needed

                    if walls:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Walls observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(walls)))}.")
                    if open_floor:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Open floor observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(open_floor)))}.")
                    if molten_lava: # Report molten lava
                        extracted_info.append(f"Molten lava (lethal, impassable) observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(molten_lava)))}.")
                    if fountains:    # Report fountains
                        extracted_info.append(f"Fountains observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(fountains)))}.")
                    if stairs_up:    # Report stairs up
                        extracted_info.append(f"Stairs up observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_up)))}.")

    # Extract inventory items and general game messages
    message_content_raw = ""
    message_match = re.search(r"message:\n([\s\S]+?)(?=\n\ncursor:|\n\nmap:|$)", observation_text)
    if message_match:
        message_content_raw = message_match.group(1)
        
    inventory_items = []
    general_messages_content = []
    
    # Process message content line by line to differentiate items from general messages
    initial_message_lines = message_content_raw.strip().split('\n')
    
    # Pattern to find individual inventory items on a line or within a line, separated by '.  '
    # Captures: 1: item_key (char), 2: item_description (text without trailing dot)
    # This regex is designed to extract a key and its description, stopping before the next period that
    # either marks the end of the item or precedes another item on the same line (separated by '  ').
    item_pattern_on_line = re.compile(r"([a-z]) - ([^.]+?)(?=\.  |\.($|\s*\n))") # The `($|\s*\n)` handles end of content or end of line.

    for line in initial_message_lines:
        line_contains_items = False
        potential_items_in_line = []
        
        # Find all individual item matches within the current line
        for match in item_pattern_on_line.finditer(line):
            item_key = match.group(1)
            item_description = match.group(2).strip()
            potential_items_in_line.append(f"Inventory item '{item_key}': {item_description}.")
            line_contains_items = True 
        
        if line_contains_items:
            # If any items were found on the line, append them to the inventory list.
            inventory_items.extend(potential_items_in_line)
        else:
            # If no inventory items were found on this specific line, consider it part of the general message.
            general_messages_content.append(line)

    cleaned_general_message = '\n'.join([l.strip() for l in general_messages_content if l.strip()])

    if inventory_items:
        extracted_info.extend(inventory_items)

    if cleaned_general_message and not cleaned_general_message.startswith("cursor:"): # Filter out cursor info which is extracted separately
        # Filter out messages that are too short/just punctuation, or contain stat line info (which are extracted separately)
        if (len(cleaned_general_message) > 3 or not re.match(r"^[. ]+$", cleaned_general_message)) and \
           not re.search(r"Dlvl:\d+.*Xp:\d+/\d+", cleaned_general_message) and \
           not re.search(r"St:\d+ Dx:\d+ Co:\d+ In:\d+ Wi:\d+ Ch:\d+ Lawful S:\d+", cleaned_general_message):
            extracted_info.append(f"Game_message: {cleaned_general_message}.")

    # Extract agent stats
    stats_match = re.search(
        r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",
        observation_text
    )
    if stats_match:
        dlvl, gold, current_hp, max_hp, current_pw, max_pw, ac, current_xp, next_level_xp = stats_match.groups()
        extracted_info.append(
            f"Agent_stats: Dlvl:{dlvl}, Gold:{gold}, HP:{current_hp}/{max_hp}, Pw:{current_pw}/{max_pw}, AC:{ac}, Xp:{current_xp}/{next_level_xp}."
        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)
```

import re

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    extracted_info = []

    # Extract agent's absolute position
    agent_x, agent_y = None, None
    # Use re.DOTALL to allow '.' to match newlines, and \s* for flexible whitespace matching
    agent_pos_match = re.search(r"cursor:\s*(?:Yourself a archeologist)?\s*\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    if agent_pos_match:
        agent_x = int(agent_pos_match.group(1))
        agent_y = int(agent_pos_match.group(2))
        extracted_info.append(f"Agent_absolute_position: ({agent_x}, {agent_y}).")

    # Extract map information if agent position is known
    if agent_x is not None and agent_y is not None:
        map_start_idx = observation_text.find("map:\n")
        if map_start_idx != -1: # Ensure map section exists
            map_content_after_tag = observation_text[map_start_idx + len("map:\n"):]

            # Define markers for the end of the map content
            map_end_marker_1 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nAgent the Digger")
            map_end_marker_2 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nSt:") # Fallback for stat line

            map_end_offset = -1
            if map_end_marker_1 != -1 and (map_end_marker_2 == -1 or map_end_marker_1 < map_end_marker_2):
                map_end_offset = map_end_marker_1
            elif map_end_marker_2 != -1:
                map_end_offset = map_end_marker_2
            
            map_section_raw = ""
            if map_end_offset != -1:
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag[:map_end_offset].strip('\n')
            else:
                # If no clear end marker like "Agent the Digger" or "St:",
                # take the rest of the content after "map:", assuming it's all map and stripping newlines.
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag.strip('\n')

            if map_section_raw:
                map_lines = map_section_raw.split('\n')
                
                # Find the agent symbol '@' on the parsed map_lines (which now include original padding)
                visual_agent_x_on_map, visual_agent_y_on_map = -1, -1
                for r_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                    if '@' in line:
                        visual_agent_x_on_map = line.find('@')
                        visual_agent_y_on_map = r_idx
                        break
                
                if visual_agent_x_on_map != -1 and visual_agent_y_on_map != -1:
                    # Calculate the offset between visual coordinates and absolute coordinates
                    offset_x = agent_x - visual_agent_x_on_map
                    offset_y = agent_y - visual_agent_y_on_map

                    walls = []
                    open_floor = []
                    molten_lava = [] # Renamed from closed_doors to molten_lava for '}' symbols
                    fountains = []
                    stairs_up = []
                    
                    for y_offset, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                        for x_offset, char in enumerate(line):
                            abs_x = x_offset + offset_x
                            abs_y = y_offset + offset_y

                            if char == '|':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'vertical'))
                            elif char == '-':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'horizontal'))
                            elif char == '.':
                                open_floor.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '}': # Classified as molten lava based on world knowledge and policy
                                molten_lava.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == 'F': # Explicitly identify 'F' as fountains
                                fountains.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '<': # Explicitly identify '<' as stairs up
                                stairs_up.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            # Add more symbols and their interpretations as needed

                    if walls:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Walls observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(walls)))}.")
                    if open_floor:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Open floor observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(open_floor)))}.")
                    if molten_lava: # Report molten lava
                        extracted_info.append(f"Molten lava (lethal, impassable) observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(molten_lava)))}.")
                    if fountains:    # Report fountains
                        extracted_info.append(f"Fountains observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(fountains)))}.")
                    if stairs_up:    # Report stairs up
                        extracted_info.append(f"Stairs up observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_up)))}.")

    # Extract inventory items and general game messages
    message_match = re.search(r"message:\n([\s\S]+?)(?=(?:\n\ncursor:|\n\nmap:|$))", observation_text)
    if message_match:
        message_content = message_match.group(1).strip()
        
        inventory_items_found = []
        # Robust regex for finding all inventory items, handling multiple on one line
        # Lookahead ensures we capture up to the next 'X - ' pattern, or end of line, or end of string.
        # This regex was slightly adjusted to ensure it captures messages like "cursor:..." when they appear.
        items_matches = list(re.finditer(r"([a-z]) - (.*?)(?=\.  [a-z] - |\.$|\.\s*$|\Z)", message_content))
        
        if items_matches:
            for match in items_matches:
                inventory_items_found.append(f"Inventory item '{match.group(1)}': {match.group(2).strip()}.")
            extracted_info.extend(inventory_items_found)
        else: # If no inventory items were found, treat the content as a general game message
            if message_content and not re.match(r"^\s*$", message_content): # Ensure it's not just whitespace
                extracted_info.append(f"Game_message: {message_content}.")

    # Extract agent stats
    stats_match = re.search(
        r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",
        observation_text
    )
    if stats_match:
        dlvl, gold, current_hp, max_hp, current_pw, max_pw, ac, current_xp, next_level_xp = stats_match.groups()
        extracted_info.append(
            f"Agent_stats: Dlvl:{dlvl}, Gold:{gold}, HP:{current_hp}/{max_hp}, Pw:{current_pw}/{max_pw}, AC:{ac}, Xp:{current_xp}/{next_level_xp}."
        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)

                            abs_y = y_offset + offset_y

                            if char == '|':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'vertical'))
                            elif char == '-':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'horizontal'))
                            elif char == '.':
                                open_floor.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '}': # Classified as molten lava based on world knowledge and policy
                                molten_lava.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                                impassable_terrain.append((abs_x, abs_y)) # Also add to impassable_terrain
                            elif char == 'F': # Explicitly identify 'F' as fountains
                                fountains.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '<': # Explicitly identify '<' as stairs up
                                stairs_up.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            # Add more symbols and their interpretations as needed

                    if walls:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Walls observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(walls)))}.")
                    if open_floor:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Open floor observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(open_floor)))}.")
                    if molten_lava: # Report molten lava
                        extracted_info.append(f"Molten lava (lethal, impassable) observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(molten_lava)))}.")
                    if fountains:    # Report fountains
                        extracted_info.append(f"Fountains observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(fountains)))}.")
                    if stairs_up:    # Report stairs up
                        extracted_info.append(f"Stairs up observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_up)))}.")
                    if impassable_terrain: # Report general impassable terrain
                        extracted_info.append(f"Impassable terrain observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(impassable_terrain)))}.")

    # Extract inventory items and general game messages
    message_match = re.search(r"message:\n([\s\S]+?)(?=(?:\n\ncursor:|\n\nmap:|$))", observation_text)
    if message_match:
        message_content = message_match.group(1).strip()
        
        inventory_items_found = []
        # Robust regex for finding all inventory items, handling multiple on one line
        # Lookahead ensures we capture up to the next 'X - ' pattern, or end of line, or end of string.
        # This regex was slightly adjusted to ensure it captures messages like "cursor:..." when they appear.
        items_matches = list(re.finditer(r"([a-z]) - (.*?)(?=\.  [a-z] - |\.$|\.\s*$|\Z)", message_content))
        
        if items_matches:
            for match in items_matches:
                inventory_items_found.append(f"Inventory item '{match.group(1)}': {match.group(2).strip()}.")
            extracted_info.extend(inventory_items_found)
        else: # If no inventory items were found, treat the content as a general game message
            if message_content and not re.match(r"^\s*$", message_content): # Ensure it's not just whitespace
                extracted_info.append(f"Game_message: {message_content}.")

    # Extract agent stats
    stats_match = re.search(
        r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",
        observation_text
    )
    if stats_match:
        dlvl, gold, current_hp, max_hp, current_pw, max_pw, ac, current_xp, next_level_xp = stats_match.groups()
        extracted_info.append(
            f"Agent_stats: Dlvl:{dlvl}, Gold:{gold}, HP:{current_hp}/{max_hp}, Pw:{current_pw}/{max_pw}, AC:{ac}, Xp:{current_xp}/{next_level_xp}."
        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)

```python
import re

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    extracted_info = []

    # Extract agent's absolute position
    agent_x, agent_y = None, None
    # Use re.DOTALL to allow '.' to match newlines, and \s* for flexible whitespace matching
    agent_pos_match = re.search(r"cursor:\s*(?:Yourself a archeologist)?\s*\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    if agent_pos_match:
        agent_x = int(agent_pos_match.group(1))
        agent_y = int(agent_pos_match.group(2))
        extracted_info.append(f"Agent_absolute_position: ({agent_x}, {agent_y}).")

    # Extract map information if agent position is known
    if agent_x is not None and agent_y is not None:
        map_start_idx = observation_text.find("map:\n")
        if map_start_idx != -1: # Ensure map section exists
            map_content_after_tag = observation_text[map_start_idx + len("map:\n"):]

            # Define markers for the end of the map content
            map_end_marker_1 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nAgent the Digger")
            map_end_marker_2 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nSt:") # Fallback for stat line

            map_end_offset = -1
            if map_end_marker_1 != -1 and (map_end_marker_2 == -1 or map_end_marker_1 < map_end_marker_2):
                map_end_offset = map_end_marker_1
            elif map_end_marker_2 != -1:
                map_end_offset = map_end_marker_2
            
            map_section_raw = ""
            if map_end_offset != -1:
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag[:map_end_offset].strip('\n')
            else:
                # If no clear end marker like "Agent the Digger" or "St:",
                # take the rest of the content after "map:", assuming it's all map and stripping newlines.
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag.strip('\n')

            if map_section_raw:
                map_lines = map_section_raw.split('\n')
                
                # Find the agent symbol '@' on the parsed map_lines (which now include original padding)
                visual_agent_x_on_map, visual_agent_y_on_map = -1, -1
                for r_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                    if '@' in line:
                        visual_agent_x_on_map = line.find('@')
                        visual_agent_y_on_map = r_idx
                        break
                
                if visual_agent_x_on_map != -1 and visual_agent_y_on_map != -1:
                    # Calculate the offset between visual coordinates and absolute coordinates
                    offset_x = agent_x - visual_agent_x_on_map
                    offset_y = agent_y - visual_agent_y_on_map

                    walls = []
                    open_floor = []
                    molten_lava = []
                    fountains = []
                    stairs_up = []
                    
                    for y_offset, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                        for x_offset, char in enumerate(line):
                            abs_x = x_offset + offset_x
                            abs_y = y_offset + offset_y

                            if char == '|':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'vertical'))
                            elif char == '-':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'horizontal'))
                            elif char == '.':
                                open_floor.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '}': # Classified as molten lava based on world knowledge and policy
                                molten_lava.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == 'F': # Explicitly identify 'F' as fountains
                                fountains.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '<': # Explicitly identify '<' as stairs up
                                stairs_up.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            # Add more symbols and their interpretations as needed

                    if walls:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Walls observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(walls)))}.")
                    if open_floor:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Open floor observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(open_floor)))}.")
                    if molten_lava: # Report molten lava, which is already known to be lethal/impassable
                        extracted_info.append(f"Molten lava (lethal, impassable) observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(molten_lava)))}.")
                    if fountains:    # Report fountains
                        extracted_info.append(f"Fountains observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(fountains)))}.")
                    if stairs_up:    # Report stairs up
                        extracted_info.append(f"Stairs up observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_up)))}.")

    # Extract inventory items and general game messages
    message_match = re.search(r"message:\n([\s\S]+?)(?=(?:\n\ncursor:|\n\nmap:|$))", observation_text)
    if message_match:
        message_content = message_match.group(1).strip()
        
        inventory_items_found = []
        # Robust regex for finding all inventory items, handling multiple on one line
        # Lookahead ensures we capture up to the next 'X - ' pattern, or end of line, or end of string.
        items_matches = list(re.finditer(r"([a-z]) - (.*?)(?=\.  [a-z] - |\.$|\.\s*$|\Z)", message_content))
        
        if items_matches:
            for match in items_matches:
                inventory_items_found.append(f"Inventory item '{match.group(1)}': {match.group(2).strip()}.")
            extracted_info.extend(inventory_items_found)
        else: # If no inventory items were found, treat the content as a general game message
            if message_content and not re.match(r"^\s*$", message_content): # Ensure it's not just whitespace
                extracted_info.append(f"Game_message: {message_content}.")

    # Extract agent stats
    stats_match = re.search(
        r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",
        observation_text
    )
    if stats_match:
        dlvl, gold, current_hp, max_hp, current_pw, max_pw, ac, current_xp, next_level_xp = stats_match.groups()
        extracted_info.append(
            f"Agent_stats: Dlvl:{dlvl}, Gold:{gold}, HP:{current_hp}/{max_hp}, Pw:{current_pw}/{max_pw}, AC:{ac}, Xp:{current_xp}/{next_level_xp}."
        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)
```

import re

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    extracted_info = []

    # Extract agent's absolute position
    agent_x, agent_y = None, None
    # Use re.DOTALL to allow '.' to match newlines, and \s* for flexible whitespace matching
    agent_pos_match = re.search(r"cursor:\s*(?:Yourself a archeologist)?\s*\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    if agent_pos_match:
        agent_x = int(agent_pos_match.group(1))
        agent_y = int(agent_pos_match.group(2))
        extracted_info.append(f"Agent_absolute_position: ({agent_x}, {agent_y}).")

    # Extract map information if agent position is known
    if agent_x is not None and agent_y is not None:
        map_start_idx = observation_text.find("map:\n")
        if map_start_idx != -1: # Ensure map section exists
            map_content_after_tag = observation_text[map_start_idx + len("map:\n"):]

            # Define markers for the end of the map content
            map_end_marker_1 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nAgent the Digger")
            map_end_marker_2 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nSt:") # Fallback for stat line

            map_end_offset = -1
            if map_end_marker_1 != -1 and (map_end_marker_2 == -1 or map_end_marker_1 < map_end_marker_2):
                map_end_offset = map_end_marker_1
            elif map_end_marker_2 != -1:
                map_end_offset = map_end_marker_2
            
            map_section_raw = ""
            if map_end_offset != -1:
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag[:map_end_offset].strip('\n')
            else:
                # If no clear end marker like "Agent the Digger" or "St:",
                # take the rest of the content after "map:", assuming it's all map and stripping newlines.
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag.strip('\n')

            if map_section_raw:
                map_lines = map_section_raw.split('\n')
                
                # Find the agent symbol '@' on the parsed map_lines (which now include original padding)
                visual_agent_x_on_map, visual_agent_y_on_map = -1, -1
                for r_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                    if '@' in line:
                        visual_agent_x_on_map = line.find('@')
                        visual_agent_y_on_map = r_idx
                        break
                
                if visual_agent_x_on_map != -1 and visual_agent_y_on_map != -1:
                    # Calculate the offset between visual coordinates and absolute coordinates
                    offset_x = agent_x - visual_agent_x_on_map
                    offset_y = agent_y - visual_agent_y_on_map

                    walls = []
                    open_floor = []
                    molten_lava = [] # Renamed from closed_doors to molten_lava for '}' symbols
                    fountains = []
                    stairs_up = []
                    
                    for y_offset, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                        for x_offset, char in enumerate(line):
                            abs_x = x_offset + offset_x
                            abs_y = y_offset + offset_y

                            if char == '|':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'vertical'))
                            elif char == '-':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'horizontal'))
                            elif char == '.':
                                open_floor.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '}': # Classified as molten lava based on world knowledge and policy
                                molten_lava.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == 'F': # Explicitly identify 'F' as fountains
                                fountains.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '<': # Explicitly identify '<' as stairs up
                                stairs_up.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            # Add more symbols and their interpretations as needed

                    if walls:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Walls observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(walls)))}.")
                    if open_floor:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Open floor observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(open_floor)))}.")
                    if molten_lava: # Report molten lava
                        extracted_info.append(f"Molten lava (lethal, impassable) observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(molten_lava)))}.")
                    if fountains:    # Report fountains
                        extracted_info.append(f"Fountains observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(fountains)))}.")
                    if stairs_up:    # Report stairs up
                        extracted_info.append(f"Stairs up observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_up)))}.")

    # Extract inventory items and general game messages
    message_match = re.search(r"message:\n([\s\S]+?)(?=(?:\n\ncursor:|\n\nmap:|$))", observation_text)
    if message_match:
        message_content = message_match.group(1).strip()
        
        inventory_items_found = []
        # Robust regex for finding all inventory items, handling multiple on one line
        # Lookahead ensures we capture up to the next 'X - ' pattern, or end of line, or end of string.
        # This regex was slightly adjusted to ensure it captures messages like "cursor:..." when they appear.
        items_matches = list(re.finditer(r"([a-z]) - (.*?)(?=\.  [a-z] - |\.$|\.\s*$|\Z)", message_content))
        
        if items_matches:
            for match in items_matches:
                inventory_items_found.append(f"Inventory item '{match.group(1)}': {match.group(2).strip()}.")
            extracted_info.extend(inventory_items_found)
        else: # If no inventory items were found, treat the content as a general game message
            if message_content and not re.match(r"^\s*$", message_content): # Ensure it's not just whitespace
                extracted_info.append(f"Game_message: {message_content}.")

    # Extract agent stats
    stats_match = re.search(
        r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",
        observation_text
    )
    if stats_match:
        dlvl, gold, current_hp, max_hp, current_pw, max_pw, ac, current_xp, next_level_xp = stats_match.groups()
        extracted_info.append(
            f"Agent_stats: Dlvl:{dlvl}, Gold:{gold}, HP:{current_hp}/{max_hp}, Pw:{current_pw}/{max_pw}, AC:{ac}, Xp:{current_xp}/{next_level_xp}."
        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)

import re

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    extracted_info = []

    # Extract agent's absolute position
    agent_x, agent_y = None, None
    # Use re.DOTALL to allow '.' to match newlines, and \s* for flexible whitespace matching
    agent_pos_match = re.search(r"cursor:\s*(?:Yourself a archeologist)?\s*\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    if agent_pos_match:
        agent_x = int(agent_pos_match.group(1))
        agent_y = int(agent_pos_match.group(2))
        extracted_info.append(f"Agent_absolute_position: ({agent_x}, {agent_y}).")

    # Extract map information if agent position is known
    if agent_x is not None and agent_y is not None:
        map_start_idx = observation_text.find("map:\n")
        if map_start_idx != -1: # Ensure map section exists
            map_content_after_tag = observation_text[map_start_idx + len("map:\n"):]

            # Define markers for the end of the map content
            map_end_marker_1 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nAgent the Digger")
            map_end_marker_2 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nSt:") # Fallback for stat line

            map_end_offset = -1
            if map_end_marker_1 != -1 and (map_end_marker_2 == -1 or map_end_marker_1 < map_end_marker_2):
                map_end_offset = map_end_marker_1
            elif map_end_marker_2 != -1:
                map_end_offset = map_end_marker_2
            
            map_section_raw = ""
            if map_end_offset != -1:
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag[:map_end_offset].strip('\n')
            else:
                # If no clear end marker like "Agent the Digger" or "St:",
                # take the rest of the content after "map:", assuming it's all map and stripping newlines.
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag.strip('\n')

            if map_section_raw:
                map_lines = map_section_raw.split('\n')
                
                # Find the agent symbol '@' on the parsed map_lines (which now include original padding)
                visual_agent_x_on_map, visual_agent_y_on_map = -1, -1
                for r_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                    if '@' in line:
                        visual_agent_x_on_map = line.find('@')
                        visual_agent_y_on_map = r_idx
                        break
                
                if visual_agent_x_on_map != -1 and visual_agent_y_on_map != -1:
                    # Calculate the offset between visual coordinates and absolute coordinates
                    offset_x = agent_x - visual_agent_x_on_map
                    offset_y = agent_y - visual_agent_y_on_map

                    walls = []
                    open_floor = []
                    molten_lava = [] # Renamed from closed_doors to molten_lava for '}' symbols
                    fountains = []
                    stairs_up = []
                    
                    for y_offset, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                        for x_offset, char in enumerate(line):
                            abs_x = x_offset + offset_x
                            abs_y = y_offset + offset_y

                            if char == '|':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'vertical'))
                            elif char == '-':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'horizontal'))
                            elif char == '.':
                                open_floor.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '}': # Classified as molten lava based on world knowledge and policy
                                molten_lava.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == 'F': # Explicitly identify 'F' as fountains
                                fountains.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '<': # Explicitly identify '<' as stairs up
                                stairs_up.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            # Add more symbols and their interpretations as needed

                    if walls:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Walls observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(walls)))}.")
                    if open_floor:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Open floor observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(open_floor)))}.")
                    if molten_lava: # Report molten lava
                        extracted_info.append(f"Molten lava (lethal, impassable) observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(molten_lava)))}.")
                    if fountains:    # Report fountains
                        extracted_info.append(f"Fountains observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(fountains)))}.")
                    if stairs_up:    # Report stairs up
                        extracted_info.append(f"Stairs up observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_up)))}.")

    # Extract inventory items and general game messages
    message_match = re.search(r"message:\n([\s\S]+?)(?=(?:\n\ncursor:|\n\nmap:|$))", observation_text)
    if message_match:
        message_content = message_match.group(1).strip()
        
        inventory_items_found = []
        # Robust regex for finding all inventory items, handling multiple on one line
        # Lookahead ensures we capture up to the next 'X - ' pattern, or end of line, or end of string.
        # This regex was slightly adjusted to ensure it captures messages like "cursor:..." when they appear.
        items_matches = list(re.finditer(r"([a-z]) - (.*?)(?=\.  [a-z] - |\.$|\.\s*$|\Z)", message_content))
        
        if items_matches:
            for match in items_matches:
                inventory_items_found.append(f"Inventory item '{match.group(1)}': {match.group(2).strip()}.")
            extracted_info.extend(inventory_items_found)
        else: # If no inventory items were found, treat the content as a general game message
            if message_content and not re.match(r"^\s*$", message_content): # Ensure it's not just whitespace
                extracted_info.append(f"Game_message: {message_content}.")

    # Extract agent stats
    stats_match = re.search(
        r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",
        observation_text
    )
    if stats_match:
        dlvl, gold, current_hp, max_hp, current_pw, max_pw, ac, current_xp, next_level_xp = stats_match.groups()
        extracted_info.append(
            f"Agent_stats: Dlvl:{dlvl}, Gold:{gold}, HP:{current_hp}/{max_hp}, Pw:{current_pw}/{max_pw}, AC:{ac}, Xp:{current_xp}/{next_level_xp}."
        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)

    stats_match = re.search(
        r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",

        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)

        agent_x = int(agent_pos_match.group(1))
        agent_y = int(agent_pos_match.group(2))
        extracted_info.append(f"Agent_absolute_position: ({agent_x}, {agent_y}).")

    # Extract map information if agent position is known
    if agent_x is not None and agent_y is not None:

            # Define markers for the end of the map content
            map_end_marker_1 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nAgent the Digger")
            map_end_marker_2 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nSt:") # Fallback for stat line

            map_end_offset = -1
            if map_end_marker_1 != -1 and (map_end_marker_2 == -1 or map_end_marker_1 < map_end_marker_2):
                map_end_offset = map_end_marker_1
            elif map_end_marker_2 != -1:
                map_end_offset = map_end_marker_2
            
            map_section_raw = ""
            if map_end_offset != -1:
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag[:map_end_offset].strip('\n')
            else:
                # If no clear end marker like "Agent the Digger" or "St:",
                # take the rest of the content after "map:", assuming it's all map and stripping newlines.
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag.strip('\n')

            if map_section_raw:
                map_lines = map_section_raw.split('\n')
                
                # Find the agent symbol '@' on the parsed map_lines (which now include original padding)
                visual_agent_x_on_map, visual_agent_y_on_map = -1, -1
                for r_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                    if '@' in line:
                        visual_agent_x_on_map = line.find('@')
                        visual_agent_y_on_map = r_idx
                        break
                
                if visual_agent_x_on_map != -1 and visual_agent_y_on_map != -1:
                    # Calculate the offset between visual coordinates and absolute coordinates
                    offset_x = agent_x - visual_agent_x_on_map
                    offset_y = agent_y - visual_agent_y_on_map

                    walls = []
                    open_floor = []
                    molten_lava = [] # Renamed from closed_doors to molten_lava for '}' symbols
                    fountains = []
                    stairs_up = []
                    
                    for y_offset, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                        for x_offset, char in enumerate(line):
                            abs_x = x_offset + offset_x
                            abs_y = y_offset + offset_y

                            if char == '|':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'vertical'))
                            elif char == '-':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'horizontal'))
                            elif char == '.':
                                open_floor.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '}': # Classified as molten lava based on world knowledge and policy
                                molten_lava.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == 'F': # Explicitly identify 'F' as fountains
                                fountains.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '<': # Explicitly identify '<' as stairs up
                                stairs_up.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            # Add more symbols and their interpretations as needed

                    if walls:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Walls observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(walls)))}.")
                    if open_floor:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Open floor observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(open_floor)))}.")
                    if molten_lava: # Report molten lava
                        extracted_info.append(f"Molten lava (lethal, impassable) observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(molten_lava)))}.")
                    if fountains:    # Report fountains
                        extracted_info.append(f"Fountains observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(fountains)))}.")
                    if stairs_up:    # Report stairs up
                        extracted_info.append(f"Stairs up observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_up)))}.")

    # Extract inventory items and general game messages
    message_match = re.search(r"message:\n([\s\S]+?)(?=(?:\n\ncursor:|\n\nmap:|$))", observation_text)
    if message_match:
        # Strip only leading/trailing whitespace from the whole message block, not inner newlines
        message_content = message_match.group(1).strip()
        
        inventory_items_found = []
        general_messages = []

        # Process message_content line by line
        # Split by newline and process each piece for inventory
        lines = message_content.split('\n')
        for line in lines:
            line = line.strip() # Strip whitespace from individual lines for cleaner matching
            if not line: # Skip empty lines
                continue
            
            # Try to match inventory item pattern for the entire line
            item_match = re.match(r"([a-z]) - (.*)", line)
            if item_match:
                inventory_items_found.append(f"Inventory item '{item_match.group(1)}': {item_match.group(2).strip()}.")
            else:
                # If not an inventory item, it's a general game message line
                general_messages.append(line)

        if inventory_items_found:
            extracted_info.extend(inventory_items_found)
        
        # Only add general game message if there are actual messages distinct from "cursor: " or empty lines
        if general_messages:
            # Filter out known non-messages like the cursor line if it gets accidentally captured
            filtered_general_messages = [msg for msg in general_messages 
                                         if not re.match(r"cursor:.*", msg) # More general cursor filter
                                         and not re.match(r"^\s*$", msg)] # Ensure not just whitespace
            
            if filtered_general_messages:
                # Preserve newlines by joining with '\n' to indicate multi-line content
                extracted_info.append(f"Game_message: {'\\n'.join(filtered_general_messages)}.")

    # Extract agent stats
    stats_match = re.search(
        r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",
        observation_text
    )
    if stats_match:
        dlvl, gold, current_hp, max_hp, current_pw, max_pw, ac, current_xp, next_level_xp = stats_match.groups()
        extracted_info.append(
            f"Agent_stats: Dlvl:{dlvl}, Gold:{gold}, HP:{current_hp}/{max_hp}, Pw:{current_pw}/{max_pw}, AC:{ac}, Xp:{current_xp}/{next_level_xp}."
        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)

import re

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    extracted_info = []

    # Extract agent's absolute position
    agent_x, agent_y = None, None
    # Use re.DOTALL to allow '.' to match newlines, and \s* for flexible whitespace matching
    agent_pos_match = re.search(r"cursor:\s*(?:Yourself a archeologist)?\s*\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    if agent_pos_match:
        agent_x = int(agent_pos_match.group(1))
        agent_y = int(agent_pos_match.group(2))
        extracted_info.append(f"Agent_absolute_position: ({agent_x}, {agent_y}).")

    # Extract map information if agent position is known
    if agent_x is not None and agent_y is not None:
        map_start_idx = observation_text.find("map:\n")
        if map_start_idx != -1: # Ensure map section exists
            map_content_after_tag = observation_text[map_start_idx + len("map:\n"):]

            # Define markers for the end of the map content
            map_end_marker_1 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nAgent the Digger")
            map_end_marker_2 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nSt:") # Fallback for stat line

            map_end_offset = -1
            if map_end_marker_1 != -1 and (map_end_marker_2 == -1 or map_end_marker_1 < map_end_marker_2):
                map_end_offset = map_end_marker_1
            elif map_end_marker_2 != -1:
                map_end_offset = map_end_marker_2
            
            map_section_raw = ""
            if map_end_offset != -1:
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag[:map_end_offset].strip('\n')
            else:
                # If no clear end marker like "Agent the Digger" or "St:",
                # take the rest of the content after "map:", assuming it's all map and stripping newlines.
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag.strip('\n')

            if map_section_raw:
                map_lines = map_section_raw.split('\n')
                
                # Find the agent symbol '@' on the parsed map_lines (which now include original padding)
                visual_agent_x_on_map, visual_agent_y_on_map = -1, -1
                for r_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                    if '@' in line:
                        visual_agent_x_on_map = line.find('@')
                        visual_agent_y_on_map = r_idx
                        break
                
                if visual_agent_x_on_map != -1 and visual_agent_y_on_map != -1:
                    # Calculate the offset between visual coordinates and absolute coordinates
                    offset_x = agent_x - visual_agent_x_on_map
                    offset_y = agent_y - visual_agent_y_on_map

                    walls = []
                    open_floor = []
                    molten_lava = [] # Renamed from closed_doors to molten_lava for '}' symbols
                    fountains = []
                    stairs_up = []
                    
                    for y_offset, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                        for x_offset, char in enumerate(line):
                            abs_x = x_offset + offset_x
                            abs_y = y_offset + offset_y

                            if char == '|':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'vertical'))
                            elif char == '-':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'horizontal'))
                            elif char == '.':
                                open_floor.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '}': # Classified as molten lava based on world knowledge and policy
                                molten_lava.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == 'F': # Explicitly identify 'F' as fountains
                                fountains.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '<': # Explicitly identify '<' as stairs up
                                stairs_up.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            # Add more symbols and their interpretations as needed

                    if walls:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Walls observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(walls)))}.")
                    if open_floor:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Open floor observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(open_floor)))}.")
                    if molten_lava: # Report molten lava
                        extracted_info.append(f"Molten lava (lethal, impassable) observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(molten_lava)))}.")
                    if fountains:    # Report fountains
                        extracted_info.append(f"Fountains observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(fountains)))}.")
                    if stairs_up:    # Report stairs up
                        extracted_info.append(f"Stairs up observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_up)))}.")

    # Extract inventory items and general game messages
    message_match = re.search(r"message:\n([\s\S]+?)(?=(?:\n\ncursor:|\n\nmap:|$))", observation_text)
    if message_match:
        message_content = message_match.group(1).strip()
        
        inventory_items_found = []
        # Robust regex for finding all inventory items, handling multiple on one line
        # Lookahead ensures we capture up to the next 'X - ' pattern, or end of line, or end of string.
        # This regex was slightly adjusted to ensure it captures messages like "cursor:..." when they appear.
        items_matches = list(re.finditer(r"([a-z]) - (.*?)(?=\.  [a-z] - |\.$|\.\s*$|\Z)", message_content))
        
        if items_matches:
            # We need to correctly parse each inventory item.
            # The previous approach had an issue combining parts if there were no periods.
            # A more robust regex for inventory items: finds a single letter, a dash, and then any text until another single letter-dash or end of string.
            # However, the previous perception output showed this was correctly parsed.
            # Let's re-run with the original inventory regex as it produced correct segmented outputs in the samples for inventory.
            
            # The issue seems to be in the last step's example, it was parsing the `cursor:` line as part of `Game_message`.
            # If `message_content` is extracted purely, and inventory items are extracted first, then what remains is game message.
            
            # Let's try iterating from the original message_content
            current_message = message_content
            extracted_items_temp = []
            
            # Extract items first
            while True:
                item_match = re.match(r"^([a-z]) - (.*?)(?:\.  |\.$|\Z)", current_message) # Match from start of string or remaining string
                if item_match:
                    letter = item_match.group(1)
                    description = item_match.group(2).strip()
                    extracted_items_temp.append(f"Inventory item '{letter}': {description}.")
                    
                    # Remove the matched item from the current_message
                    current_message = current_message[len(item_match.group(0)):].strip()
                    if current_message.startswith('.'): # Handle specific cases like ".  k - "
                        current_message = current_message[1:].strip()
                else:
                    break
            
            if extracted_items_temp:
                extracted_info.extend(extracted_items_temp)
            
            # Whatever is left in current_message after inventory extraction is a general game message
            if current_message and not re.match(r"^\s*$", current_message):
                # Preserve newlines in raw game messages for better readability
                extracted_info.append(f"Game_message: {current_message}.")
        else: # If no inventory items were found with the `items_matches` regex originally.
            if message_content and not re.match(r"^\s*$", message_content): # Ensure it's not just whitespace
                extracted_info.append(f"Game_message: {message_content}.")

    # Extract agent stats
    stats_match = re.search(
        r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",
        observation_text
    )
    if stats_match:
        dlvl, gold, current_hp, max_hp, current_pw, max_pw, ac, current_xp, next_level_xp = stats_match.groups()
        extracted_info.append(
            f"Agent_stats: Dlvl:{dlvl}, Gold:{gold}, HP:{current_hp}/{max_hp}, Pw:{current_pw}/{max_pw}, AC:{ac}, Xp:{current_xp}/{next_level_xp}."
        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)

import re

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    extracted_info = []

    # Extract agent's absolute position
    agent_x, agent_y = None, None
    # Use re.DOTALL to allow '.' to match newlines, and \s* for flexible whitespace matching
    agent_pos_match = re.search(r"cursor:\s*(?:Yourself a archeologist)?\s*\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    if agent_pos_match:
        agent_x = int(agent_pos_match.group(1))
        agent_y = int(agent_pos_match.group(2))
        extracted_info.append(f"Agent_absolute_position: ({agent_x}, {agent_y}).")

    # Extract map information if agent position is known
    if agent_x is not None and agent_y is not None:
        map_start_idx = observation_text.find("map:\n")
        if map_start_idx != -1: # Ensure map section exists
            map_content_after_tag = observation_text[map_start_idx + len("map:\n"):]

            # Define markers for the end of the map content
            map_end_marker_1 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nAgent the Digger")
            map_end_marker_2 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nSt:") # Fallback for stat line

            map_end_offset = -1
            if map_end_marker_1 != -1 and (map_end_marker_2 == -1 or map_end_marker_1 < map_end_marker_2):
                map_end_offset = map_end_marker_1
            elif map_end_marker_2 != -1:
                map_end_offset = map_end_marker_2
            
            map_section_raw = ""
            if map_end_offset != -1:
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag[:map_end_offset].strip('\n')
            else:
                # If no clear end marker like "Agent the Digger" or "St:",
                # take the rest of the content after "map:", assuming it's all map and stripping newlines.
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag.strip('\n')

            if map_section_raw:
                map_lines = map_section_raw.split('\n')
                
                # Find the agent symbol '@' on the parsed map_lines (which now include original padding)
                visual_agent_x_on_map, visual_agent_y_on_map = -1, -1
                for r_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                    if '@' in line:
                        visual_agent_x_on_map = line.find('@')
                        visual_agent_y_on_map = r_idx
                        break
                
                if visual_agent_x_on_map != -1 and visual_agent_y_on_map != -1:
                    # Calculate the offset between visual coordinates and absolute coordinates
                    offset_x = agent_x - visual_agent_x_on_map
                    offset_y = agent_y - visual_agent_y_on_map

                    walls = []
                    open_floor = []
                    molten_lava = [] 
                    fountains = []
                    stairs_up = []
                    
                    for y_offset, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                        for x_offset, char in enumerate(line):
                            abs_x = x_offset + offset_x
                            abs_y = y_offset + offset_y

                            if char == '|':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'vertical'))
                            elif char == '-':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'horizontal'))
                            elif char == '.':
                                open_floor.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '}': 
                                molten_lava.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == 'F': 
                                fountains.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '<': 
                                stairs_up.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            # Add more symbols and their interpretations as needed

                    if walls:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Walls observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(walls)))}.")
                    if open_floor:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Open floor observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(open_floor)))}.")
                    if molten_lava: 
                        extracted_info.append(f"Molten lava (lethal, impassable) observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(molten_lava)))}.")
                    if fountains:    
                        extracted_info.append(f"Fountains observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(fountains)))}.")
                    if stairs_up:    
                        extracted_info.append(f"Stairs up observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_up)))}.")

    # Extract inventory items and general game messages
    message_match = re.search(r"message:\n([\s\S]+?)(?=(?:\n\ncursor:|\n\nmap:|$))", observation_text)
    if message_match:
        message_content = message_match.group(1).strip()
        
        inventory_items_found = []
        # Robust regex for finding all inventory items, handling multiple on one line
        # Capture item letter, then the description until a period followed by whitespace, or end of string.
        items_matches = list(re.finditer(r"([a-z]) - ([^.]*?)(?:\.\s*|$)", message_content))
        
        if items_matches:
            for match in items_matches:
                # Strip leading/trailing whitespace from the captured description
                inventory_items_found.append(f"Inventory item '{match.group(1)}': {match.group(2).strip()}.")
            extracted_info.extend(inventory_items_found)
        else: # If no inventory items were found, treat the content as a general game message
            if message_content and not re.match(r"^\s*$", message_content): # Ensure it's not just whitespace
                extracted_info.append(f"Game_message: {message_content}.")

    # Extract agent stats
    stats_match = re.search(
        r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",
        observation_text
    )
    if stats_match:
        dlvl, gold, current_hp, max_hp, current_pw, max_pw, ac, current_xp, next_level_xp = stats_match.groups()
        extracted_info.append(
            f"Agent_stats: Dlvl:{dlvl}, Gold:{gold}, HP:{current_hp}/{max_hp}, Pw:{current_pw}/{max_pw}, AC:{ac}, Xp:{current_xp}/{next_level_xp}."
        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)

import re

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    extracted_info = []

    # Extract agent's absolute position
    agent_x, agent_y = None, None
    agent_pos_match = re.search(r"cursor:\s*(?:Yourself a archeologist)?\s*\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    if agent_pos_match:
        agent_x = int(agent_pos_match.group(1))
        agent_y = int(agent_pos_match.group(2))
        extracted_info.append(f"Agent_absolute_position: ({agent_x}, {agent_y}).")

    # Extract map information if agent position is known
    if agent_x is not None and agent_y is not None:
        map_start_idx = observation_text.find("map:\n")
        if map_start_idx != -1: # Ensure map section exists
            map_content_after_tag = observation_text[map_start_idx + len("map:\n"):]

            # Define markers for the end of the map content
            map_end_marker_1 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nAgent the Digger")
            map_end_marker_2 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nSt:") # Fallback for stat line

            map_end_offset = -1
            if map_end_marker_1 != -1 and (map_end_marker_2 == -1 or map_end_marker_1 < map_end_marker_2):
                map_end_offset = map_end_marker_1
            elif map_end_marker_2 != -1:
                map_end_offset = map_end_marker_2
            
            map_section_raw = ""
            if map_end_offset != -1:
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag[:map_end_offset].strip('\n')
            else:
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag.strip('\n')

            if map_section_raw:
                map_lines = map_section_raw.split('\n')
                
                visual_agent_x_on_map, visual_agent_y_on_map = -1, -1
                for r_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                    if '@' in line:
                        visual_agent_x_on_map = line.find('@')
                        visual_agent_y_on_map = r_idx
                        break
                
                if visual_agent_x_on_map != -1 and visual_agent_y_on_map != -1:
                    offset_x = agent_x - visual_agent_x_on_map
                    offset_y = agent_y - visual_agent_y_on_map

                    walls = []
                    open_floor = []
                    molten_lava = []
                    fountains = []
                    stairs_up = []
                    
                    for y_offset, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                        for x_offset, char in enumerate(line):
                            abs_x = x_offset + offset_x
                            abs_y = y_offset + offset_y

                            if char == '|':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'vertical'))
                            elif char == '-':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'horizontal'))
                            elif char == '.':
                                open_floor.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '}':
                                molten_lava.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == 'F':
                                fountains.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '<':
                                stairs_up.append((abs_x, abs_y))

                    if walls:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Walls observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(walls)))}.")
                    if open_floor:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Open floor observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(open_floor)))}.")
                    if molten_lava:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Molten lava (lethal, impassable) observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(molten_lava)))}.")
                    if fountains:    
                        extracted_info.append(f"Fountains observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(fountains)))}.")
                    if stairs_up:    
                        extracted_info.append(f"Stairs up observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_up)))}.")

    # Extract inventory items and general game messages
    message_content = ""
    message_match = re.search(r"message:\n([\s\S]*?)(?=(?:\n\ncursor:|\n\nmap:|$))", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    if message_match:
        message_content = message_match.group(1).strip()

    inventory_items_list = []
    clean_message_parts = []

    # Use a regex to find all inventory items and correctly extract them
    # It looks for 'letter - description' followed by either a period and spaces, or end of string/line.
    # The `re.DOTALL` flag only applies to `.` not `\s`. It's better to explicitly match `\s*` or `[\s\S]`
    # for the description part to handle multiline items, but for common inventory lines, `.*?` until a specific boundary works.
    item_regex = re.compile(r"([a-z])\s*-\s*(.*?)(?=\.\s*[a-z]\s*-\s*|\.\s*$|\s*$|[\n])", re.DOTALL) 
    
    # Process the message content to separate inventory items from other messages
    last_end = 0
    temp_message_content = message_content # Use a temporary variable for iteration
    
    for m in item_regex.finditer(temp_message_content):
        # Extract any non-item message string that appeared *before* this item
        pre_item_message = temp_message_content[last_end:m.start()].strip()
        if pre_item_message:
            clean_message_parts.append(pre_item_message)
        
        # Extract the inventory item itself
        # Group 1 is the item key, Group 2 is the description
        inventory_items_list.append(f"Inventory item '{m.group(1)}': {m.group(2).strip()}.")
        last_end = m.end()

    # After iterating, append any remaining message content that appears *after* the last item
    if last_end < len(temp_message_content):
        post_item_message = temp_message_content[last_end:].strip()
        if post_item_message:
            clean_message_parts.append(post_item_message)

    if inventory_items_list:
        extracted_info.extend(inventory_items_list)
    
    # Only add a game message if there is actual content after processing inventory
    combined_game_message = ' '.join(clean_message_parts).strip()
    if combined_game_message:
        extracted_info.append(f"Game_message: {combined_game_message}.")


    # Extract agent stats
    stats_match = re.search(
        r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",
        observation_text
    )
    if stats_match:
        dlvl, gold, current_hp, max_hp, current_pw, max_pw, ac, current_xp, next_level_xp = stats_match.groups()
        extracted_info.append(
            f"Agent_stats: Dlvl:{dlvl}, Gold:{gold}, HP:{current_hp}/{max_hp}, Pw:{current_pw}/{max_pw}, AC:{ac}, Xp:{current_xp}/{next_level_xp}."
        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)

```python
import re

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    extracted_info = []

    # Extract agent's absolute position
    agent_x, agent_y = None, None
    # Use re.DOTALL to allow '.' to match newlines, and \s* for flexible whitespace matching
    agent_pos_match = re.search(r"cursor:\s*(?:Yourself a archeologist)?\s*\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    if agent_pos_match:
        agent_x = int(agent_pos_match.group(1))
        agent_y = int(agent_pos_match.group(2))
        extracted_info.append(f"Agent_absolute_position: ({agent_x}, {agent_y}).")

    # Extract map information if agent position is known
    if agent_x is not None and agent_y is not None:
        map_start_idx = observation_text.find("map:\n")
        if map_start_idx != -1: # Ensure map section exists
            map_content_after_tag = observation_text[map_start_idx + len("map:\n"):]

            # Define markers for the end of the map content
            map_end_marker_1 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nAgent the Digger")
            map_end_marker_2 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nSt:") # Fallback for stat line

            map_end_offset = -1
            if map_end_marker_1 != -1 and (map_end_marker_2 == -1 or map_end_marker_1 < map_end_marker_2):
                map_end_offset = map_end_marker_1
            elif map_end_marker_2 != -1:
                map_end_offset = map_end_marker_2
            
            map_section_raw = ""
            if map_end_offset != -1:
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag[:map_end_offset].strip('\n')
            else:
                # If no clear end marker like "Agent the Digger" or "St:",
                # take the rest of the content after "map:", assuming it's all map and stripping newlines.
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag.strip('\n')

            if map_section_raw:
                map_lines = map_section_raw.split('\n')
                
                # Find the agent symbol '@' on the parsed map_lines (which now include original padding)
                visual_agent_x_on_map, visual_agent_y_on_map = -1, -1
                for r_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                    if '@' in line:
                        visual_agent_x_on_map = line.find('@')
                        visual_agent_y_on_map = r_idx
                        break
                
                if visual_agent_x_on_map != -1 and visual_agent_y_on_map != -1:
                    # Calculate the offset between visual coordinates and absolute coordinates
                    offset_x = agent_x - visual_agent_x_on_map
                    offset_y = agent_y - visual_agent_y_on_map

                    walls = []
                    open_floor = []
                    molten_lava = [] # Renamed from closed_doors to molten_lava for '}' symbols
                    fountains = []
                    stairs_up = []
                    
                    for y_offset, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                        for x_offset, char in enumerate(line):
                            abs_x = x_offset + offset_x
                            abs_y = y_offset + offset_y

                            if char == '|':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'vertical'))
                            elif char == '-':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'horizontal'))
                            elif char == '.':
                                open_floor.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '}': # Classified as molten lava based on world knowledge and policy
                                molten_lava.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == 'F': # Explicitly identify 'F' as fountains
                                fountains.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '<': # Explicitly identify '<' as stairs up
                                stairs_up.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            # Add more symbols and their interpretations as needed

                    if walls:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Walls observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(walls)))}.")
                    if open_floor:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Open floor observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(open_floor)))}.")
                    if molten_lava: # Report molten lava
                        extracted_info.append(f"Molten lava (lethal, impassable) observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(molten_lava)))}.")
                    if fountains:    # Report fountains
                        extracted_info.append(f"Fountains observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(fountains)))}.")
                    if stairs_up:    # Report stairs up
                        extracted_info.append(f"Stairs up observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_up)))}.")

    # Extract inventory items and general game messages
    message_content = ""
    # Adjusted regex to use '*' instead of '+' for initial capture to allow for truly empty message blocks
    message_match = re.search(r"message:\n([\s\S]*?)(?=(?:\n\ncursor:|\n\nmap:|$))", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    if message_match:
        message_content = message_match.group(1).strip()

    inventory_items_list = []
    clean_message_parts = []
    
    # Process the message content to separate inventory items from other messages
    last_end = 0
    
    # Regex for an inventory item: 'single_lowercase_letter - description'
    # The description is non-greedy (.*?) and ends before another item starts,
    # or the end of the message_content.
    # The ending lookahead is crucial:
    #   - `(?=\.\s*[a-z]\s*-\s*)`: matches next item separated by '.  letter - '
    #   - `(?=\.\s*$)`: matches end of message_content if it ends with '. '
    #   - `(?=\s*$|\n)`: matches end of message_content if it ends with whitespace/newline
    # The `\s*` after the description handles optional trailing spaces before the actual end of the item/lookahead.
    item_regex = re.compile(r"([a-z])\s*-\s*(.*?)(?=\.\s*[a-z]\s*-\s*|\.\s*$|\s*$|[\n])", re.DOTALL) 

    # Find all inventory items in the message_content
    for m in item_regex.finditer(message_content):
        # Add any non-item message text that appeared *before* this item
        pre_item_message = message_content[last_end:m.start()].strip()
        if pre_item_message:
            clean_message_parts.append(pre_item_message)
        
        # Add the extracted inventory item
        inventory_items_list.append(f"Inventory item '{m.group(1)}': {m.group(2).strip()}.")
        last_end = m.end()

    # Add any remaining message text that appeared *after* the last item
    if last_end < len(message_content):
        post_item_message = message_content[last_end:].strip()
        if post_item_message:
            clean_message_parts.append(post_item_message)

    if inventory_items_list:
        extracted_info.extend(inventory_items_list)
    
    # Combine any non-inventory message parts into a single game message
    combined_game_message = ' '.join(clean_message_parts).strip()
    if combined_game_message: # Only add if there's actual message content
        extracted_info.append(f"Game_message: {combined_game_message}.")

    # Extract agent stats
    stats_match = re.search(
        r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",
        observation_text
    )
    if stats_match:
        dlvl, gold, current_hp, max_hp, current_pw, max_pw, ac, current_xp, next_level_xp = stats_match.groups()
        extracted_info.append(
            f"Agent_stats: Dlvl:{dlvl}, Gold:{gold}, HP:{current_hp}/{max_hp}, Pw:{current_pw}/{max_pw}, AC:{ac}, Xp:{current_xp}/{next_level_xp}."
        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)
