```python
import re

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    extracted_info = []

    # Extract agent's absolute position
    agent_x, agent_y = None, None
    agent_pos_match = re.search(r"cursor:\s*(?:.*?)\s*\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    if agent_pos_match:
        agent_x = int(agent_pos_match.group(1))
        agent_y = int(agent_pos_match.group(2))
        extracted_info.append(f"Agent_absolute_position: ({agent_x}, {agent_y}).")

    # Extract map information
    map_start_idx = observation_text.find("map:\n")
    if map_start_idx != -1:
        map_content_start_abs_idx = map_start_idx + len("map:\n")
        
        abs_map_end_marker_agent_digger = observation_text.find("\nAgent the Digger", map_content_start_abs_idx)
        abs_map_end_marker_stat_line = observation_text.find("\nDlvl:", map_content_start_abs_idx)
        
        potential_abs_end_indices = []
        if abs_map_end_marker_agent_digger != -1:
            potential_abs_end_indices.append(abs_map_end_marker_agent_digger)
        if abs_map_end_marker_stat_line != -1:
            potential_abs_end_indices.append(abs_map_end_marker_stat_line)
        
        min_abs_end_idx = -1
        if potential_abs_end_indices:
            min_abs_end_idx = min(potential_abs_end_indices)

        map_section_raw = ""
        if min_abs_end_idx != -1:
            map_section_raw = observation_text[map_content_start_abs_idx:min_abs_end_idx].strip('\n')
        else:
            map_section_raw = observation_text[map_content_start_abs_idx:].strip('\n')

    if map_section_raw and any(c in map_section_raw for c in ['@', '|', '-', '.', '}', 'F', '<']):
        map_lines = map_section_raw.split('\n')
        
        visual_agent_x_on_map, visual_agent_y_on_map = -1, -1
        for r_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
            if '@' in line:
                visual_agent_x_on_map = line.find('@')
                visual_agent_y_on_map = r_idx
                break
        
        if visual_agent_x_on_map != -1 and visual_agent_y_on_map != -1 and agent_x is not None and agent_y is not None:
            offset_x = agent_x - visual_agent_x_on_map
            offset_y = agent_y - visual_agent_y_on_map

            walls = []
            open_floor = []
            molten_lava = []
            fountains = []
            stairs_up = []
            
            for y_offset, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                for x_offset, char in enumerate(line):
                    abs_x = x_offset + offset_x
                    abs_y = y_offset + offset_y

                    if char == '|':
                        walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'vertical'))
                    elif char == '-':
                        walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'horizontal'))
                    elif char == '.':
                        open_floor.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                    elif char == '}':
                        molten_lava.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                    elif char == 'F':
                        fountains.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                    elif char == '<':
                        stairs_up.append((abs_x, abs_y))

            if walls:
                extracted_info.append(f"Walls observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(walls)))}.")
            if open_floor:
                extracted_info.append(f"Open floor observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(open_floor)))}.")
            if molten_lava:
                extracted_info.append(f"Molten lava (lethal, impassable) observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(molten_lava)))}.")
            if fountains:
                extracted_info.append(f"Fountains observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(fountains)))}.")
            if stairs_up:
                extracted_info.append(f"Stairs up observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_up)))}.")

    # Extract inventory items and general game messages
    message_content_match = re.search(r"message:\n([\s\S]+?)(?=\n\ncursor:|\n\nmap:|\nAgent the Digger|\nDlvl:|\Z)", observation_text)
    
    if message_content_match:
        full_message_block = message_content_match.group(1).strip()
        
        inventory_items_found_list = []
        game_messages_list = []
        
        # Regex for inventory items. Looks for `letter - description.` followed by at least one space,
        # or `letter - description.` at the end of the line.
        # This will be applied line by line, allowing specific handling.
        inventory_item_regex = re.compile(r"([a-z]) - (.*?)(?:\. +|$|\.$)")

        lines = full_message_block.split('\n')
        for line in lines:
            line = line.strip()
            if not line:
                continue
            
            # Use findall to get all items on a single line, e.g., "j - a horn. k - a pine wand."
            all_items_on_line = list(inventory_item_regex.finditer(line))
            
            if all_items_on_line:
                temp_inventory_list = []
                last_end = 0
                for match in all_items_on_line:
                    item_char = match.group(1)
                    item_desc = match.group(2)
                    temp_inventory_list.append(f"Inventory item '{item_char}': {item_desc.strip()}.")
                    last_end = match.end()
                
                # If the entire line was consumed by inventory items, add to inventory list
                # This checks if there's any non-whitespace garbage left after the last item match
                if re.match(r"^\s*$", line[last_end:]): # All remaining characters are whitespace
                    inventory_items_found_list.extend(temp_inventory_list)
                else: # Line contains non-inventory text, so it's a general message
                    game_messages_list.append(line)
            else: # Line does not contain any inventory items
                if not re.match(r"^\s*cursor:", line): # Filter out cursor lines if they appear in message block
                    game_messages_list.append(line)

        if inventory_items_found_list:
            extracted_info.extend(sorted(list(set(inventory_items_found_list))))

        if game_messages_list:
            cleaned_game_messages = [msg for msg in game_messages_list if msg.strip()]
            if cleaned_game_messages:
                extracted_info.append(f"Game_message: {' '.join(cleaned_game_messages).strip()}.")

    # Extract agent stats
    stats_match = re.search(
        r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",
        observation_text
    )
    if stats_match:
        dlvl, gold, current_hp, max_hp, current_pw, max_pw, ac, current_xp, next_level_xp = stats_match.groups()
        extracted_info.append(
            f"Agent_stats: Dlvl:{dlvl}, Gold:{gold}, HP:{current_hp}/{max_hp}, Pw:{current_pw}/{max_pw}, AC:{ac}, Xp:{current_xp}/{next_level_xp}."
        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)
```

The regex for inventory items `r"([a-z]) - (.*?)(?:\. +|$|\.$)"` in `re.finditer` is critical.
`(?:\. +|$)` matches `. ` (many spaces) or end of string.
`|(?:\.$)` matches `.` at *end of line before newline*.

Let's test it with `j - a horn.  k - a pine wand.` (two spaces after `horn.`)
- `re.finditer(r"([a-z]) - (.*?)(?:\. +|$|\.$)", line)`
- First match: `j - a horn`. Group 1 `j`, Group 2 `a horn`. The `\. +` consumed `.  `.
- The `finditer` will then continue from *after* the `.` and spaces were consumed. So it would not find `k - a pine wand.`. This is why `finditer` is tricky with consuming lookaheads or non-capturing groups.

```python
            map_section_raw = observation_text[map_content_start_abs_idx:].strip('\n')

                else: # Line contains both inventory and other message text

            else: # No inventory pattern found on this line

        if inventory_items_found_list:
            extracted_info.extend(sorted(list(set(inventory_items_found_list))))

            cleaned_game_messages = [msg for msg in game_messages_list if msg.strip()]
            if cleaned_game_messages:

        r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",

        )
```

```python
            map_section_raw = observation_text[map_content_start_abs_idx:].strip('\n')

    # Only process map if it contains discernible map characters (not just a death message or empty)
    # and has a decent length, or if agent is present.
    if map_section_raw and any(c in map_section_raw for c in ['@', '|', '-', '.', '}', 'F', '<']):
        map_lines = map_section_raw.split('\n')
        
        # Find the agent symbol '@' on the parsed map_lines
        visual_agent_x_on_map, visual_agent_y_on_map = -1, -1
        for r_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
            if '@' in line:
                visual_agent_x_on_map = line.find('@')
                visual_agent_y_on_map = r_idx
                break
        
        # If agent's visual position is found on the map, calculate absolute coordinates
        # and if agent's absolute coordinates are also known
        if visual_agent_x_on_map != -1 and visual_agent_y_on_map != -1 and agent_x is not None and agent_y is not None:
            # Calculate the offset between visual coordinates and absolute coordinates
            offset_x = agent_x - visual_agent_x_on_map
            offset_y = agent_y - visual_agent_y_on_map

            walls = []
            open_floor = []
            molten_lava = []
            fountains = []
            stairs_up = []
            
            for y_offset, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                for x_offset, char in enumerate(line):
                    abs_x = x_offset + offset_x
                    abs_y = y_offset + offset_y

                    if char == '|':
                        walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'vertical'))
                    elif char == '-':
                        walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'horizontal'))
                    elif char == '.':
                        open_floor.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                    elif char == '}': # Classified as molten lava based on world knowledge and policy
                        molten_lava.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                    elif char == 'F': # Explicitly identify 'F' as fountains
                        fountains.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                    elif char == '<': # Explicitly identify '<' as stairs up
                        stairs_up.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                    # Add more symbols and their interpretations as needed

            if walls:
                extracted_info.append(f"Walls observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(walls)))}.")
            if open_floor:
                extracted_info.append(f"Open floor observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(open_floor)))}.")
            if molten_lava: # Report molten lava
                extracted_info.append(f"Molten lava (lethal, impassable) observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(molten_lava)))}.")
            if fountains:    # Report fountains
                extracted_info.append(f"Fountains observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(fountains)))}.")
            if stairs_up:    # Report stairs up
                extracted_info.append(f"Stairs up observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_up)))}.")

    # Extract inventory items and general game messages
    # The regex for the message block should end before other main sections like 'cursor:', 'map:', 'Agent the Digger', or 'Dlvl:'.
    message_content_match = re.search(r"message:\n([\s\S]+?)(?=\n\ncursor:|\n\nmap:|\nAgent the Digger|\nDlvl:|\Z)", observation_text)
    
    if message_content_match:
        full_message_block = message_content_match.group(1).strip()
        
        inventory_items_found_list = []
        game_messages_list = []
        
        # This regex looks for:
        # ([a-z]) - a single lowercase letter followed by ' - ' (captured as item_char)
        # (.*?) - any characters non-greedily (captured as item_desc)
        # Followed by a positive lookahead for:
        #   (?:\. [a-z] - ) - a dot, single space, then another item pattern (e.g., ". k - ")
        #   |(?:\.$) - OR a dot at the very end of the line
        #   |(?:$) - OR the very end of the line (no dot)
        # This allows re.finditer to find all non-overlapping items on a single line.
        inventory_item_pattern = re.compile(r"([a-z]) - (.*?)(?=(?:\. [a-z] - )|(?:\.$)|(?:$))")

        lines = full_message_block.split('\n')
        for line in lines:
            line = line.strip()
            if not line:
                continue
            
            items_found_in_line = []
            last_idx_after_matches = 0 # To track consumed portion of the line by inventory matches
            
            # Use finditer to get all items on a single line, e.g., "j - a horn. k - a pine wand."
            # The indices of the matches help determine if the entire line was an inventory list.
            matches_on_line = list(re.finditer(inventory_item_pattern, line))

            if matches_on_line:
                for match in matches_on_line:
                    item_char = match.group(1)
                    item_desc = match.group(2)
                    items_found_in_line.append(f"Inventory item '{item_char}': {item_desc.strip()}.")
                    # Update last_idx_after_matches to the end of the current match
                    last_idx_after_matches = match.end()
                
                # Check if the entire line, after consuming all inventory items, is just whitespace.
                # If so, the line is considered purely inventory.
                remaining_text_after_inventory_matches = line[last_idx_after_matches:].strip()
                if not remaining_text_after_inventory_matches:
                    inventory_items_found_list.extend(items_found_in_line)
                else: # Line contained both inventory and other message text, treat as general message
                    game_messages_list.append(line)
            else: # No inventory pattern found on this line
                # Filter out lines that are purely whitespace or contain only irrelevant characters.
                if not re.match(r"^\s*cursor:", line): # Filter out cursor lines if they appear in message block
                    game_messages_list.append(line)

        if inventory_items_found_list:
            # Deduplicate items and sort for consistent output
            extracted_info.extend(sorted(list(set(inventory_items_found_list))))

        if game_messages_list:
            # Join non-empty game messages.
            cleaned_game_messages = [msg for msg in game_messages_list if msg.strip()]
            if cleaned_game_messages:
                # One final filter to ensure no 'cursor:' lines despite previous attempts
                final_game_messages = []
                for msg in cleaned_game_messages:
                    if not re.match(r"^\s*cursor:", msg, re.IGNORECASE):
                        final_game_messages.append(msg)
                
                if final_game_messages:
                    extracted_info.append(f"Game_message: {' '.join(final_game_messages).strip()}.")


    # Extract agent stats
    # Stats line is typically at the very end
    stats_match = re.search(
        r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",
        observation_text
    )
    if stats_match:
        dlvl, gold, current_hp, max_hp, current_pw, max_pw, ac, current_xp, next_level_xp = stats_match.groups()
        extracted_info.append(
            f"Agent_stats: Dlvl:{dlvl}, Gold:{gold}, HP:{current_hp}/{max_hp}, Pw:{current_pw}/{max_pw}, AC:{ac}, Xp:{current_xp}/{next_level_xp}."
        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)
```

```python
        inventory_items_found.append(f"Inventory item '{item_key}': {item_description}.")
        # Replace the matched item string with empty string in processed_message_content
        # to isolate remaining actual messages
        processed_message_content = processed_message_content.replace(item_match.group(0), "", 1) # Only replace once
        
    # After extracting inventory items, whatever remains in `processed_message_content` is the game message.
    # Clean up whitespace and empty lines.
    cleaned_general_message = '\n'.join([line.strip() for line in processed_message_content.split('\n') if line.strip()])
    
    # Exclude the "cursor: Yourself a archeologist (x=X, y=Y)" part from game messages
    # as it's extracted separately and can cause duplicates or misinterpretations.
    if cleaned_general_message:
        # Filter out cursor-related messages which are now handled by agent_pos_match
        if not cleaned_general_message.startswith("cursor:"):
            # Also, a message consisting only of "..." or similar minimal punctuation is not useful.
            if len(cleaned_general_message) > 3 or not re.match(r"^[. ]+$", cleaned_general_message):
                general_messages.append(f"Game_message: {cleaned_general_message}.")

    if inventory_items_found:
        extracted_info.extend(inventory_items_found)
    if general_messages:
        extracted_info.extend(general_messages)

    # Extract agent stats
    stats_match = re.search(
        r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",
        observation_text
    )
    if stats_match:
        dlvl, gold, current_hp, max_hp, current_pw, max_pw, ac, current_xp, next_level_xp = stats_match.groups()
        extracted_info.append(
            f"Agent_stats: Dlvl:{dlvl}, Gold:{gold}, HP:{current_hp}/{max_hp}, Pw:{current_pw}/{max_pw}, AC:{ac}, Xp:{current_xp}/{next_level_xp}."
        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)
```

```python
def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:

                            elif char == '}':

                    if walls:

                    if open_floor:

    # The item string could be "j - a horn." or "j - a horn.  k - a pine wand." (if on the same line)
    # The previous regex (r"([a-z]) - (.*?)(?:\.  (?=[a-z] -)|\.$|\n)") was actually decent, but it needs to be applied carefully.

    # Try with a modified item regex in the full message block.
    # It must start on a new line or directly after a period and two spaces, to distinguish from arbitrary text.
    
    # Resetting the message extraction logic.
    inventory_items = []
    temp_message_content = message_content_raw.strip()

    # Pattern for individual inventory items.
    # It accounts for items possibly appearing on the same line, separated by '.  ', or ending a line.
    # It *must* start with a letter and a dash. The `(?:^\s*|\.  )` ensures it's either at the beginning of a line (after optional whitespace)
    # or after a preceding item.
    item_pattern_refined = re.compile(r"(?:^\s*|\.  )([a-z]) - ([^.\n]+?\.)", re.MULTILINE)

    # Use finditer to get all matches. The regex is designed to only capture `[a-z] - item_desc.`
    # For "j - a horn.  k - a pine wand."
    # First match: `j - a horn.` (group 1: 'j', group 2: 'a horn.')

    # This should yield correct separate items.

    item_spans = []
    for match in item_pattern_refined.finditer(temp_message_content):

        item_description = match.group(2).strip()
        inventory_items.append(f"Inventory item '{item_key}': {item_description}.")
        item_spans.append(match.span()) # Store the start and end indices of the match

    # Reconstruct the general message by removing the item spans
    # Sort spans in reverse to avoid index shifting issues during removal
    item_spans.sort(key=lambda x: x[0], reverse=True)

    message_chars = list(temp_message_content)
    
    for start, end in item_spans:

        # This will remove exact matches of "j - a horn." or "k - a pine wand."
        for i in range(start, end):
            message_chars[i] = ' ' # Replace with space or empty string

    remaining_message = "".join(message_chars)
    
    # Clean up excess whitespace and newline characters
    cleaned_general_message_lines = [line.strip() for line in remaining_message.strip().split('\n') if line.strip()]
    cleaned_general_message = '\n'.join(cleaned_general_message_lines)

    # Add inventory items
    if inventory_items:
        extracted_info.extend(inventory_items)

    # Add general messages, excluding cursor lines or purely whitespace/punctuation messages
    if cleaned_general_message and not cleaned_general_message.startswith("cursor:"):
        # Further filter out messages like "..." or empty strings after stripping
        if len(cleaned_general_message) > 3 or not re.match(r"^[. ]+$", cleaned_general_message):
            if "St:15 Dx:9 Co:14 In:14 Wi:14 Ch:9 Lawful S:0" not in cleaned_general_message: # Avoid including stat line if it gets appended here
                general_messages.append(f"Game_message: {cleaned_general_message}.")

    if general_messages:
        extracted_info.extend(general_messages)

    # Extract agent stats, ensuring not to conflict with game message
    stats_match = re.search(
        r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",
        observation_text
    )
    if stats_match:
        dlvl, gold, current_hp, max_hp, current_pw, max_pw, ac, current_xp, next_level_xp = stats_match.groups()
        extracted_info.append(
            f"Agent_stats: Dlvl:{dlvl}, Gold:{gold}, HP:{current_hp}/{max_hp}, Pw:{current_pw}/{max_pw}, AC:{ac}, Xp:{current_xp}/{next_level_xp}."
        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)
```

```python
    # Store tuples of (start_index, end_index) for matched items to remove them from the general message easily
    item_matches_to_remove = []
    
    temp_message_content = message_content_raw.strip() # Strip outer whitespace for clean matching
    
    for match in item_pattern_final.finditer(temp_message_content):
        item_key = match.group(1)
        item_description = match.group(2).strip()
        inventory_items.append(f"Inventory item '{item_key}': {item_description}.")
        
        # Calculate precise span including the matched letter, description, and its trailing dot for removal
        # The span needs to be of the exact item string, including the ending period and subsequent two spaces if present.
        # This requires careful reconstruction as 'match.group(0)' includes `(?:^\s*|\.  )`
        
        # To get the full item string including its terminating period.
        # This is a bit tricky with lookaheads. Let's find the explicit "letter - description."
        # Use simpler approach again: extract all and rebuild.
        
    temp_message_lines = message_content_raw.split('\n')
    remaining_message_lines = []
    all_items_processed = False
    
    # This approach assumes all inventory items are at the very start of the message section,
    # and subsequent lines/text are general messages. This seems to be how the examples are structured.
    # Let's collect all potential inventory lines first.
    
    # Iteratively extract items from the beginning of the raw message content, if possible
    current_message_residue = message_content_raw.strip()
    
    # This regex matches a single inventory item statement, including the final period and possible next item delimiter '  '.
    # It tries to find 'j - a horn.  k - a pine wand.' then extract 'j - a horn' and 'k - a pine wand' separately
    # Pattern to capture an item followed by its delimiter or end of line/string.
    item_full_pattern = re.compile(r"^\s*([a-z]) - ([^.\n]+?\.)(?:\s{2}([a-z]) - ([^.\n]+?\.)|\s*$|\n)", re.MULTILINE|re.DOTALL)
    
    # Attempt to split multi-item lines explicitly.
    # A single regex with `findall` is likely better here.
    
    # Find all simple "letter - description." patterns.
    # The regex should capture (key, desc) and be robust against non-item text.
    # The previous example `j - a horn. k - a pine wand.` implies items are always `key - description.`
    # And there can be multiple on one line, separated by ` `
    
    found_item_strings = []
    
    # Revised strategy:
    # 1. Look for lines that look like a list of items `key - description.`
    # 2. Extract these items.
    # 3. What's left is game_message.
    
    # This pattern captures an item part (key - description.)
    individual_item_pattern = re.compile(r"([a-z]) - ([^.\n]+?\.)")
    
    processed_message_lines = []
    for line in message_content_raw.strip().split('\n'):
        line_has_items = False
        remaining_line = line
        
        # Find all items in the current line
        line_items = []
        for match in individual_item_pattern.finditer(line):
            item_key = match.group(1)
            item_description = match.group(2).strip('.') # Strip the trailing period for the output format
            inventory_items.append(f"Inventory item '{item_key}': {item_description}.")
            line_has_items = True

        # If the line contained items, it's considered an "inventory line" and its content is removed from the general message consideration.
        # Otherwise, the line is preserved for the general message.
        if not line_has_items:
            processed_message_lines.append(line)
            
    cleaned_general_message = '\n'.join([l.strip() for l in processed_message_lines if l.strip()])

    if inventory_items:
        extracted_info.extend(inventory_items)

    if cleaned_general_message and not cleaned_general_message.startswith("cursor:"):
        if len(cleaned_general_message) > 3 or not re.match(r"^[. ]+$", cleaned_general_message):
             # Ensure not to re-add stat line if it was accidentally put into general message content
            if "Dlvl:" not in cleaned_general_message and "St:" not in cleaned_general_message: 
                general_messages.append(f"Game_message: {cleaned_general_message}.")

    if general_messages:
        extracted_info.extend(general_messages)

    # Extract agent stats
    stats_match = re.search(
        r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",
        observation_text
    )
    if stats_match:
        dlvl, gold, current_hp, max_hp, current_pw, max_pw, ac, current_xp, next_level_xp = stats_match.groups()
        extracted_info.append(
            f"Agent_stats: Dlvl:{dlvl}, Gold:{gold}, HP:{current_hp}/{max_hp}, Pw:{current_pw}/{max_pw}, AC:{ac}, Xp:{current_xp}/{next_level_xp}."
        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)
```

```python
import re

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    extracted_info = []

    # Extract agent's absolute position
    agent_x, agent_y = None, None
    agent_pos_match = re.search(r"cursor:\s*(?:Yourself a archeologist)?\s*\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    if agent_pos_match:
        agent_x = int(agent_pos_match.group(1))
        agent_y = int(agent_pos_match.group(2))
        extracted_info.append(f"Agent_absolute_position: ({agent_x}, {agent_y}).")

    # Extract map information if agent position is known
    if agent_x is not None and agent_y is not None:
        map_start_idx = observation_text.find("map:\n")
        if map_start_idx != -1:
            map_content_after_tag = observation_text[map_start_idx + len("map:\n"):]

            map_end_marker_1 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nAgent the Digger")
            map_end_marker_2 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nSt:")
            
            map_end_offset = -1
            if map_end_marker_1 != -1 and (map_end_marker_2 == -1 or map_end_marker_1 < map_end_marker_2):
                map_end_offset = map_end_marker_1
            elif map_end_marker_2 != -1:
                map_end_offset = map_end_marker_2
            
            map_section_raw = ""
            if map_end_offset != -1:
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag[:map_end_offset].strip('\n')
            else:
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag.strip('\n') 

            if map_section_raw:
                map_lines = map_section_raw.split('\n')
                
                visual_agent_x_on_map, visual_agent_y_on_map = -1, -1
                for r_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                    if '@' in line:
                        visual_agent_x_on_map = line.find('@')
                        visual_agent_y_on_map = r_idx
                        break
                
                if visual_agent_x_on_map != -1 and visual_agent_y_on_map != -1:
                    offset_x = agent_x - visual_agent_x_on_map
                    offset_y = agent_y - visual_agent_y_on_map

                    walls = []
                    open_floor = []
                    molten_lava = []
                    fountains = []
                    stairs_up = []
                    
                    for y_offset, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                        for x_offset, char in enumerate(line):
                            abs_x = x_offset + offset_x
                            abs_y = y_offset + offset_y

                            if char == '|':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'vertical'))
                            elif char == '-':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'horizontal'))
                            elif char == '.':
                                open_floor.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '}':
                                molten_lava.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == 'F':
                                fountains.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '<':
                                stairs_up.append((abs_x, abs_y))

                    if walls:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Walls observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(walls)))}.")
                    if open_floor:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Open floor observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(open_floor)))}.")
                    if molten_lava:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Molten lava (lethal, impassable) observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(molten_lava)))}.")
                    if fountains:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Fountains observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(fountains)))}.")
                    if stairs_up:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Stairs up observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_up)))}.")

    # Extract inventory items and general game messages
    message_content_raw = ""
    message_match = re.search(r"message:\n([\s\S]+?)(?=\n\ncursor:|\n\nmap:|$)", observation_text)
    if message_match:
        message_content_raw = message_match.group(1)

    inventory_items = []
    general_messages_content = []
    
    # Pattern to find individual inventory items on a line or within a line, separated by '.  '
    # Captures: 1: item_key (char), 2: item_description (text without trailing dot)
    # The lookahead `(?=\.  ?[a-z] -|\.)` ensures the description ends before a dot which is followed by
    # another potential item or just ends the full item description. 
    # Use re.findall to get all occurrences, then we can remove them.
    # The output format for items is 'j': a horn. (without trailing period).
    
    # Let's try to match full inventory lines (multiple items per line allowed)
    initial_message_lines = message_content_raw.strip().split('\n')
    
    item_pattern_on_line = re.compile(r"([a-z]) - ([^.]+?)(?=\.  |[.])") # Match key and desc up to a period or '  '
    
    for line in initial_message_lines:
        line_contains_pure_items = False
        potential_items_in_line = []
        for match in item_pattern_on_line.finditer(line):
            item_key = match.group(1)
            item_description = match.group(2).strip()
            potential_items_in_line.append(f"Inventory item '{item_key}': {item_description}.")
            
            # Check if the entire line possibly consists solely of items (like "j - a. k - b.")
            # This is heuristic, but helps distinguish item lists from arbitrary text
            # A simple way to check: if the first non-space character is `[a-z] - ` and it ends with a period,
            # it's likely an item line. This will be done below to filter general messages.

        if potential_items_in_line:
            # If any items were found on the line, assume it's an item-listing line
            # and consume it into inventory_items. This heuristic means that a line
            # might have some other text but if it has `x - item.`, we extract it.
            # This is based on the assumption that inventory is always cleanly presented.
            inventory_items.extend(potential_items_in_line)
        else:
            # If no items explicitly matched, it's a general message line.
            general_messages_content.append(line)

    cleaned_general_message = '\n'.join([l.strip() for l in general_messages_content if l.strip()])

    if inventory_items:
        extracted_info.extend(inventory_items)

    if cleaned_general_message and not cleaned_general_message.startswith("cursor:"): # Filter out cursor info
        if len(cleaned_general_message) > 3 or not re.match(r"^[. ]+$", cleaned_general_message): # Filter short/empty messages
            # Filter out known stat lines that occasionally appear in the message section
            if not re.search(r"Dlvl:\d+.*Xp:\d+/\d+", cleaned_general_message): 
                extracted_info.append(f"Game_message: {cleaned_general_message}.")

    # Extract agent stats
    stats_match = re.search(
        r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",
        observation_text
    )
    if stats_match:
        dlvl, gold, current_hp, max_hp, current_pw, max_pw, ac, current_xp, next_level_xp = stats_match.groups()
        extracted_info.append(
            f"Agent_stats: Dlvl:{dlvl}, Gold:{gold}, HP:{current_hp}/{max_hp}, Pw:{current_pw}/{max_pw}, AC:{ac}, Xp:{current_xp}/{next_level_xp}."
        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)


```

Let's test the `item_pattern_on_line` regex: `r"([a-z]) - ([^.]+?)(?=\.  |[.])"` on `j - a horn. k - a pine wand.`
1. First `match`: `group 1='j'`, `group 2='a horn'`. The `(?=\.  |[\.])` matches the `.` after `horn`. Output: "Inventory item 'j': a horn."
2. Second `match`: `group 1='k'`, `group 2='a pine wand'`. The `(?=\.  |[\.])` matches the `.` after `wand`. Output: "Inventory item 'k': a pine wand."
This correctly identifies both items and provides the description without the trailing period. This looks good.

For the empty message in step 4: `message: \n\ncursor:...`:
`message_content_raw` will receive an empty string from `message_match.group(1)`.
`initial_message_lines` will be `[]` after `.strip().split('\n')`.
The loop `for line in initial_message_lines:` won't execute.
`cleaned_general_message` will be `""`.
So `if cleaned_general_message` will be false, preventing the `Game_message` output. This looks correct.

For the death message in step 9:
`message_content_raw` will contain the multi-line death message.
`item_pattern_on_line` will not find `([a-z]) - ` in the death message lines.
`potential_items_in_line` will be empty for all those lines.
So these lines will correctly be added to `general_messages_content`.
`cleaned_general_message` will be the entire death message, which will be outputted as a `Game_message`.
This also seems correct.

The logic for extracting item descriptions without the trailing period, and the robustness against `cursor:` lines and `stat:` lines, seems generally improved.
The `item_pattern_on_line` also needs `re.DOTALL` if item descriptions can contain newlines, which is uncommon for simple inventories. For now, assuming descriptions are single-line. If needed, `[^.]` could be `[\s\S]` to allow newlines, but `[^.]+?` implies non-greedy capture until the next period. Let's keep it concise following the examples.

```python
           not re.search(r"Dlvl:\d+.*Xp:\d+/\d+", cleaned_general_message) and \
           not re.search(r"St:\d+ Dx:\d+ Co:\d+ In:\d+ Wi:\d+ Ch:\d+ Lawful S:\d+", cleaned_general_message):
            extracted_info.append(f"Game_message: {cleaned_general_message}.")

    # Extract agent stats
    stats_match = re.search(
        r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",
        observation_text
    )
    if stats_match:
        dlvl, gold, current_hp, max_hp, current_pw, max_pw, ac, current_xp, next_level_xp = stats_match.groups()
        extracted_info.append(
            f"Agent_stats: Dlvl:{dlvl}, Gold:{gold}, HP:{current_hp}/{max_hp}, Pw:{current_pw}/{max_pw}, AC:{ac}, Xp:{current_xp}/{next_level_xp}."
        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)
```
</updated_perception>

import re

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    extracted_info = []

    # Extract agent's absolute position
    agent_x, agent_y = None, None
    # Use re.DOTALL to allow '.' to match newlines, and \s* for flexible whitespace matching
    agent_pos_match = re.search(r"cursor:\s*(?:Yourself a archeologist)?\s*\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    if agent_pos_match:
        agent_x = int(agent_pos_match.group(1))
        agent_y = int(agent_pos_match.group(2))
        extracted_info.append(f"Agent_absolute_position: ({agent_x}, {agent_y}).")

    # Extract map information if agent position is known
    if agent_x is not None and agent_y is not None:
        map_start_idx = observation_text.find("map:\n")
        if map_start_idx != -1: # Ensure map section exists
            map_content_after_tag = observation_text[map_start_idx + len("map:\n"):]

            # Define markers for the end of the map content
            map_end_marker_1 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nAgent the Digger")
            map_end_marker_2 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nSt:") # Fallback for stat line

            map_end_offset = -1
            if map_end_marker_1 != -1 and (map_end_marker_2 == -1 or map_end_marker_1 < map_end_marker_2):
                map_end_offset = map_end_marker_1
            elif map_end_marker_2 != -1:
                map_end_offset = map_end_marker_2
            
            map_section_raw = ""
            if map_end_offset != -1:
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag[:map_end_offset].strip('\n')
            else:
                # If no clear end marker like "Agent the Digger" or "St:",
                # take the rest of the content after "map:", assuming it's all map and stripping newlines.
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag.strip('\n')

            if map_section_raw:
                map_lines = map_section_raw.split('\n')
                
                # Find the agent symbol '@' on the parsed map_lines (which now include original padding)
                visual_agent_x_on_map, visual_agent_y_on_map = -1, -1
                for r_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                    if '@' in line:
                        visual_agent_x_on_map = line.find('@')
                        visual_agent_y_on_map = r_idx
                        break
                
                if visual_agent_x_on_map != -1 and visual_agent_y_on_map != -1:
                    # Calculate the offset between visual coordinates and absolute coordinates
                    offset_x = agent_x - visual_agent_x_on_map
                    offset_y = agent_y - visual_agent_y_on_map

                    walls = []
                    open_floor = []
                    molten_lava = []
                    fountains = []
                    stairs_up = []
                    
                    for y_offset, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                        for x_offset, char in enumerate(line):
                            abs_x = x_offset + offset_x
                            abs_y = y_offset + offset_y

                            if char == '|':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'vertical'))
                            elif char == '-':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'horizontal'))
                            elif char == '.':
                                open_floor.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '}': # Classified as molten lava based on world knowledge and policy
                                molten_lava.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == 'F': # Explicitly identify 'F' as fountains
                                fountains.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '<': # Explicitly identify '<' as stairs up
                                stairs_up.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            # Add more symbols and their interpretations as needed

                    if walls:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Walls observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(walls)))}.")
                    if open_floor:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Open floor observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(open_floor)))}.")
                    if molten_lava: # Report molten lava
                        extracted_info.append(f"Molten lava (lethal, impassable) observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(molten_lava)))}.")
                    if fountains:    # Report fountains
                        extracted_info.append(f"Fountains observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(fountains)))}.")
                    if stairs_up:    # Report stairs up
                        extracted_info.append(f"Stairs up observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_up)))}.")

    # Extract inventory items and general game messages
    message_content_raw = ""
    message_match = re.search(r"message:\n([\s\S]+?)(?=\n\ncursor:|\n\nmap:|$)", observation_text)
    if message_match:
        message_content_raw = message_match.group(1)
        
    inventory_items = []
    general_messages_content = []
    
    # Process message content line by line to differentiate items from general messages
    initial_message_lines = message_content_raw.strip().split('\n')
    
    # Pattern to find individual inventory items on a line or within a line, separated by '.  '
    # Captures: 1: item_key (char), 2: item_description (text without trailing dot)
    # This regex is designed to extract a key and its description, stopping before the next period that
    # either marks the end of the item or precedes another item on the same line (separated by '  ').
    item_pattern_on_line = re.compile(r"([a-z]) - ([^.]+?)(?=\.  |\.($|\s*\n))") # The `($|\s*\n)` handles end of content or end of line.

    for line in initial_message_lines:
        line_contains_items = False
        potential_items_in_line = []
        
        # Find all individual item matches within the current line
        for match in item_pattern_on_line.finditer(line):
            item_key = match.group(1)
            item_description = match.group(2).strip()
            potential_items_in_line.append(f"Inventory item '{item_key}': {item_description}.")
            line_contains_items = True 
        
        if line_contains_items:
            # If any items were found on the line, append them to the inventory list.
            inventory_items.extend(potential_items_in_line)
        else:
            # If no inventory items were found on this specific line, consider it part of the general message.
            general_messages_content.append(line)

    cleaned_general_message = '\n'.join([l.strip() for l in general_messages_content if l.strip()])

    if inventory_items:
        extracted_info.extend(inventory_items)

    if cleaned_general_message and not cleaned_general_message.startswith("cursor:"): # Filter out cursor info which is extracted separately
        # Filter out messages that are too short/just punctuation, or contain stat line info (which are extracted separately)
        if (len(cleaned_general_message) > 3 or not re.match(r"^[. ]+$", cleaned_general_message)) and \
           not re.search(r"Dlvl:\d+.*Xp:\d+/\d+", cleaned_general_message) and \
           not re.search(r"St:\d+ Dx:\d+ Co:\d+ In:\d+ Wi:\d+ Ch:\d+ Lawful S:\d+", cleaned_general_message):
            extracted_info.append(f"Game_message: {cleaned_general_message}.")

    # Extract agent stats
    stats_match = re.search(
        r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",
        observation_text
    )
    if stats_match:
        dlvl, gold, current_hp, max_hp, current_pw, max_pw, ac, current_xp, next_level_xp = stats_match.groups()
        extracted_info.append(
            f"Agent_stats: Dlvl:{dlvl}, Gold:{gold}, HP:{current_hp}/{max_hp}, Pw:{current_pw}/{max_pw}, AC:{ac}, Xp:{current_xp}/{next_level_xp}."
        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)

```python
import re

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    extracted_info = []

    # Extract agent's absolute position
    agent_x, agent_y = None, None
    # Use re.DOTALL to allow '.' to match newlines, and \s* for flexible whitespace matching
    agent_pos_match = re.search(r"cursor:\s*(?:Yourself a archeologist)?\s*\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    if agent_pos_match:
        agent_x = int(agent_pos_match.group(1))
        agent_y = int(agent_pos_match.group(2))
        extracted_info.append(f"Agent_absolute_position: ({agent_x}, {agent_y}).")

    # Extract map information if agent position is known
    if agent_x is not None and agent_y is not None:
        map_start_idx = observation_text.find("map:\n")
        if map_start_idx != -1: # Ensure map section exists
            map_content_after_tag = observation_text[map_start_idx + len("map:\n"):]

            # Define markers for the end of the map content
            map_end_marker_1 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nAgent the Digger")
            map_end_marker_2 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nSt:") # Fallback for stat line

            map_end_offset = -1
            if map_end_marker_1 != -1 and (map_end_marker_2 == -1 or map_end_marker_1 < map_end_marker_2):
                map_end_offset = map_end_marker_1
            elif map_end_marker_2 != -1:
                map_end_offset = map_end_marker_2
            
            map_section_raw = ""
            if map_end_offset != -1:
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag[:map_end_offset].strip('\n')
            else:
                # If no clear end marker like "Agent the Digger" or "St:",
                # take the rest of the content after "map:", assuming it's all map and stripping newlines.
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag.strip('\n')

            if map_section_raw:
                map_lines = map_section_raw.split('\n')
                
                # Find the agent symbol '@' on the parsed map_lines (which now include original padding)
                visual_agent_x_on_map, visual_agent_y_on_map = -1, -1
                for r_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                    if '@' in line:
                        visual_agent_x_on_map = line.find('@')
                        visual_agent_y_on_map = r_idx
                        break
                
                if visual_agent_x_on_map != -1 and visual_agent_y_on_map != -1:
                    # Calculate the offset between visual coordinates and absolute coordinates
                    offset_x = agent_x - visual_agent_x_on_map
                    offset_y = agent_y - visual_agent_y_on_map

                    walls = []
                    open_floor = []
                    molten_lava = []
                    fountains = []
                    stairs_up = []
                    
                    for y_offset, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                        for x_offset, char in enumerate(line):
                            abs_x = x_offset + offset_x
                            abs_y = y_offset + offset_y

                            if char == '|':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'vertical'))
                            elif char == '-':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'horizontal'))
                            elif char == '.':
                                open_floor.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '}': # Classified as molten lava based on world knowledge and policy
                                molten_lava.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == 'F': # Explicitly identify 'F' as fountains
                                fountains.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '<': # Explicitly identify '<' as stairs up
                                stairs_up.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            # Add more symbols and their interpretations as needed

                    if walls:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Walls observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(walls)))}.")
                    if open_floor:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Open floor observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(open_floor)))}.")
                    if molten_lava: # Report molten lava
                        extracted_info.append(f"Molten lava (lethal, impassable) observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(molten_lava)))}.")
                    if fountains:    # Report fountains
                        extracted_info.append(f"Fountains observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(fountains)))}.")
                    if stairs_up:    # Report stairs up
                        extracted_info.append(f"Stairs up observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_up)))}.")

    # Extract inventory items and general game messages
    message_content_raw = ""
    message_match = re.search(r"message:\n([\s\S]+?)(?=\n\ncursor:|\n\nmap:|$)", observation_text)
    if message_match:
        message_content_raw = message_match.group(1)
        
    inventory_items = []
    general_messages_content = []
    
    # Process message content line by line to differentiate items from general messages
    initial_message_lines = message_content_raw.strip().split('\n')
    
    # Pattern to find individual inventory items on a line or within a line, separated by '.  '
    # Captures: 1: item_key (char), 2: item_description (text without trailing dot)
    # This regex is designed to extract a key and its description, stopping before the next period that
    # either marks the end of the item or precedes another item on the same line (separated by '  ').
    item_pattern_on_line = re.compile(r"([a-z]) - ([^.]+?)(?=\.  |\.($|\s*\n))") # The `($|\s*\n)` handles end of content or end of line.

    for line in initial_message_lines:
        line_contains_items = False
        potential_items_in_line = []
        
        # Find all individual item matches within the current line
        for match in item_pattern_on_line.finditer(line):
            item_key = match.group(1)
            item_description = match.group(2).strip()
            potential_items_in_line.append(f"Inventory item '{item_key}': {item_description}.")
            line_contains_items = True 
        
        if line_contains_items:
            # If any items were found on the line, append them to the inventory list.
            inventory_items.extend(potential_items_in_in_line)
        else:
            # If no inventory items were found on this specific line, consider it part of the general message.
            general_messages_content.append(line)

    cleaned_general_message = '\n'.join([l.strip() for l in general_messages_content if l.strip()])

    if inventory_items:
        extracted_info.extend(inventory_items)

    if cleaned_general_message and not cleaned_general_message.startswith("cursor:"): # Filter out cursor info which is extracted separately
        # Filter out messages that are too short/just punctuation, or contain stat line info (which are extracted separately)
        if (len(cleaned_general_message) > 3 or not re.match(r"^[. ]+$", cleaned_general_message)) and \
           not re.search(r"Dlvl:\d+.*Xp:\d+/\d+", cleaned_general_message) and \
           not re.search(r"St:\d+ Dx:\d+ Co:\d+ In:\d+ Wi:\d+ Ch:\d+ Lawful S:\d+", cleaned_general_message):
            extracted_info.append(f"Game_message: {cleaned_general_message}.")

    # Extract agent stats
    stats_match = re.search(
        r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",
        observation_text
    )
    if stats_match:
        dlvl, gold, current_hp, max_hp, current_pw, max_pw, ac, current_xp, next_level_xp = stats_match.groups()
        extracted_info.append(
            f"Agent_stats: Dlvl:{dlvl}, Gold:{gold}, HP:{current_hp}/{max_hp}, Pw:{current_pw}/{max_pw}, AC:{ac}, Xp:{current_xp}/{next_level_xp}."
        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)
```

```python
import re

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    extracted_info = []

    # Extract agent's absolute position
    agent_x, agent_y = None, None
    # Use re.DOTALL to allow '.' to match newlines, and \s* for flexible whitespace matching
    agent_pos_match = re.search(r"cursor:\s*(?:Yourself a archeologist)?\s*\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    if agent_pos_match:
        agent_x = int(agent_pos_match.group(1))
        agent_y = int(agent_pos_match.group(2))
        extracted_info.append(f"Agent_absolute_position: ({agent_x}, {agent_y}).")

    # Extract map information if agent position is known
    if agent_x is not None and agent_y is not None:
        map_start_idx = observation_text.find("map:\n")
        if map_start_idx != -1: # Ensure map section exists
            map_content_after_tag = observation_text[map_start_idx + len("map:\n"):]

            # Define markers for the end of the map content
            map_end_marker_1 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nAgent the Digger")
            map_end_marker_2 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nSt:") # Fallback for stat line

            map_end_offset = -1
            if map_end_marker_1 != -1 and (map_end_marker_2 == -1 or map_end_marker_1 < map_end_marker_2):
                map_end_offset = map_end_marker_1
            elif map_end_marker_2 != -1:
                map_end_offset = map_end_marker_2
            
            map_section_raw = ""
            if map_end_offset != -1:
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag[:map_end_offset].strip('\n')
            else:
                # If no clear end marker like "Agent the Digger" or "St:",
                # take the rest of the content after "map:", assuming it's all map and stripping newlines.
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag.strip('\n')

            if map_section_raw:
                map_lines = map_section_raw.split('\n')
                
                # Find the agent symbol '@' on the parsed map_lines (which now include original padding)
                visual_agent_x_on_map, visual_agent_y_on_map = -1, -1
                for r_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                    if '@' in line:
                        visual_agent_x_on_map = line.find('@')
                        visual_agent_y_on_map = r_idx
                        break
                
                if visual_agent_x_on_map != -1 and visual_agent_y_on_map != -1:
                    # Calculate the offset between visual coordinates and absolute coordinates
                    offset_x = agent_x - visual_agent_x_on_map
                    offset_y = agent_y - visual_agent_y_on_map

                    walls = []
                    open_floor = []
                    molten_lava = []
                    fountains = []
                    stairs_up = []
                    
                    for y_offset, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                        for x_offset, char in enumerate(line):
                            abs_x = x_offset + offset_x
                            abs_y = y_offset + offset_y

                            if char == '|':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'vertical'))
                            elif char == '-':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'horizontal'))
                            elif char == '.':
                                open_floor.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '}': # Classified as molten lava based on world knowledge and policy
                                molten_lava.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == 'F': # Explicitly identify 'F' as fountains
                                fountains.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '<': # Explicitly identify '<' as stairs up
                                stairs_up.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            # Add more symbols and their interpretations as needed

                    if walls:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Walls observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(walls)))}.")
                    if open_floor:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Open floor observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(open_floor)))}.")
                    if molten_lava: # Report molten lava
                        extracted_info.append(f"Molten lava (lethal, impassable) observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(molten_lava)))}.")
                    if fountains:    # Report fountains
                        extracted_info.append(f"Fountains observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(fountains)))}.")
                    if stairs_up:    # Report stairs up
                        extracted_info.append(f"Stairs up observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_up)))}.")

    # Extract inventory items and general game messages
    message_content_raw = ""
    message_match = re.search(r"message:\n([\s\S]+?)(?=\n\ncursor:|\n\nmap:|$)", observation_text)
    if message_match:
        message_content_raw = message_match.group(1)
        
    inventory_items = []
    general_messages_content = []
    
    # Process message content line by line to differentiate items from general messages
    initial_message_lines = message_content_raw.strip().split('\n')
    
    # Pattern to find individual inventory items on a line or within a line, separated by '.  '
    # Captures: 1: item_key (char), 2: item_description (text without trailing dot)
    # This regex is designed to extract a key and its description, stopping before the next period that
    # either marks the end of the item or precedes another item on the same line (separated by '  ').
    item_pattern_on_line = re.compile(r"([a-z]) - ([^.]+?)(?=\.  |\.($|\s*\n))") # The `($|\s*\n)` handles end of content or end of line.

    for line in initial_message_lines:
        line_contains_items = False
        potential_items_in_line = [] # Corrected variable name from potential_items_in_in_line
        
        # Find all individual item matches within the current line
        for match in item_pattern_on_line.finditer(line):
            item_key = match.group(1)
            item_description = match.group(2).strip()
            potential_items_in_line.append(f"Inventory item '{item_key}': {item_description}.")
            line_contains_items = True 
        
        if line_contains_items:
            # If any items were found on the line, append them to the inventory list.
            inventory_items.extend(potential_items_in_line)
        else:
            # If no inventory items were found on this specific line, consider it part of the general message.
            general_messages_content.append(line)

    cleaned_general_message = '\n'.join([l.strip() for l in general_messages_content if l.strip()])

    if inventory_items:
        extracted_info.extend(inventory_items)

    if cleaned_general_message and not cleaned_general_message.startswith("cursor:"): # Filter out cursor info which is extracted separately
        # Filter out messages that are too short/just punctuation, or contain stat line info (which are extracted separately)
        if (len(cleaned_general_message) > 3 or not re.match(r"^[. ]+$", cleaned_general_message)) and \
           not re.search(r"Dlvl:\d+.*Xp:\d+/\d+", cleaned_general_message) and \
           not re.search(r"St:\d+ Dx:\d+ Co:\d+ In:\d+ Wi:\d+ Ch:\d+ Lawful S:\d+", cleaned_general_message):
            extracted_info.append(f"Game_message: {cleaned_general_message}.")

    # Extract agent stats
    stats_match = re.search(
        r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",
        observation_text
    )
    if stats_match:
        dlvl, gold, current_hp, max_hp, current_pw, max_pw, ac, current_xp, next_level_xp = stats_match.groups()
        extracted_info.append(
            f"Agent_stats: Dlvl:{dlvl}, Gold:{gold}, HP:{current_hp}/{max_hp}, Pw:{current_pw}/{max_pw}, AC:{ac}, Xp:{current_xp}/{next_level_xp}."
        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)
```

```python
import re

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    extracted_info = []

    # Extract agent's absolute position
    agent_x, agent_y = None, None
    # Use re.DOTALL to allow '.' to match newlines, and \s* for flexible whitespace matching
    agent_pos_match = re.search(r"cursor:\s*(?:Yourself a archeologist|Yourself a priestess)?\s*\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    if agent_pos_match:
        agent_x = int(agent_pos_match.group(1))
        agent_y = int(agent_pos_match.group(2))
        extracted_info.append(f"Agent_absolute_position: ({agent_x}, {agent_y}).")

    # Extract map information if agent position is known
    if agent_x is not None and agent_y is not None:
        map_start_idx = observation_text.find("map:\n")
        if map_start_idx != -1: # Ensure map section exists
            map_content_after_tag = observation_text[map_start_idx + len("map:\n"):]

            # Define markers for the end of the map content
            map_end_marker_1 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nAgent the Digger")
            map_end_marker_2 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nAgent the Aspirant") # New agent type
            map_end_marker_3 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nSt:") # Fallback for stat line

            map_end_offset = -1
            if map_end_marker_1 != -1 and (map_end_marker_2 == -1 or map_end_marker_1 < map_end_marker_2) and (map_end_marker_3 == -1 or map_end_marker_1 < map_end_marker_3):
                map_end_offset = map_end_marker_1
            elif map_end_marker_2 != -1 and (map_end_marker_1 == -1 or map_end_marker_2 < map_end_marker_1) and (map_end_marker_3 == -1 or map_end_marker_2 < map_end_marker_3):
                map_end_offset = map_end_marker_2
            elif map_end_marker_3 != -1:
                map_end_offset = map_end_marker_3
            
            map_section_raw = ""
            if map_end_offset != -1:
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag[:map_end_offset].strip('\n')
            else:
                # If no clear end marker, take the rest of the content after "map:", assuming it's all map.
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag.strip('\n')

            if map_section_raw:
                map_lines = map_section_raw.split('\n')
                
                # Find the agent symbol '@' on the parsed map_lines
                visual_agent_x_on_map, visual_agent_y_on_map = -1, -1
                for r_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                    if '@' in line:
                        visual_agent_x_on_map = line.find('@')
                        visual_agent_y_on_map = r_idx
                        break
                
                if visual_agent_x_on_map != -1 and visual_agent_y_on_map != -1:
                    # Calculate the offset between visual coordinates and absolute coordinates
                    offset_x = agent_x - visual_agent_x_on_map
                    offset_y = agent_y - visual_agent_y_on_map

                    walls = []
                    open_floor = []
                    molten_lava = []
                    fountains = []
                    stairs_up = []
                    
                    for y_offset, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                        for x_offset, char in enumerate(line):
                            abs_x = x_offset + offset_x
                            abs_y = y_offset + offset_y

                            if char == '|':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'vertical'))
                            elif char == '-':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'horizontal'))
                            elif char == '.':
                                open_floor.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '}': # Classified as molten lava
                                molten_lava.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == 'F': # Explicitly identify 'F' as fountains
                                fountains.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '<': # Explicitly identify '<' as stairs up
                                stairs_up.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            # Add more symbols and their interpretations as needed

                    if walls:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Walls observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(walls)))}.")
                    if open_floor:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Open floor observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(open_floor)))}.")
                    if molten_lava: # Report molten lava
                        extracted_info.append(f"Molten lava (lethal, impassable) observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(molten_lava)))}.")
                    if fountains:    # Report fountains
                        extracted_info.append(f"Fountains observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(fountains)))}.")
                    if stairs_up:    # Report stairs up
                        extracted_info.append(f"Stairs up observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_up)))}.")

    # Extract inventory items and general game messages
    message_content_raw = ""
    message_match = re.search(r"message:\n([\s\S]+?)(?=\n\ncursor:|\n\nmap:|$)", observation_text)
    if message_match:
        message_content_raw = message_match.group(1)
        
    inventory_items = []
    general_messages_content = []
    
    item_pattern_on_line = re.compile(r"([a-z]) - ([^.]+?)(?=\.  |\.($|\s*\n))")

    for line in message_content_raw.strip().split('\n'):
        line_contains_items = False
        potential_items_in_line = []
        
        for match in item_pattern_on_line.finditer(line):
            item_key = match.group(1)
            item_description = match.group(2).strip()
            potential_items_in_line.append(f"Inventory item '{item_key}': {item_description}.")
            line_contains_items = True 
        
        if line_contains_items:
            inventory_items.extend(potential_items_in_line)
        else:
            general_messages_content.append(line)

    cleaned_general_message = '\n'.join([l.strip() for l in general_messages_content if l.strip()])

    if inventory_items:
        extracted_info.extend(inventory_items)

    if cleaned_general_message and not cleaned_general_message.startswith("cursor:"): # Filter out cursor info
        if (len(cleaned_general_message) > 3 or not re.match(r"^[. ]+$", cleaned_general_message)) and \
           not re.search(r"Dlvl:\d+.*Xp:\d+/\d+", cleaned_general_message) and \
           not re.search(r"St:\d+ Dx:\d+ Co:\d+ In:\d+ Wi:\d+ Ch:\d+ Lawful S:\d+", cleaned_general_message):
            extracted_info.append(f"Game_message: {cleaned_general_message}.")

    # Extract agent stats
    # Updated regex to also capture "Agent the Aspirant" for new character type if needed and general stat line
    stats_match = re.search(
        r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",
        observation_text
    )
    if stats_match:
        dlvl, gold, current_hp, max_hp, current_pw, max_pw, ac, current_xp, next_level_xp = stats_match.groups()
        extracted_info.append(
            f"Agent_stats: Dlvl:{dlvl}, Gold:{gold}, HP:{current_hp}/{max_hp}, Pw:{current_pw}/{max_pw}, AC:{ac}, Xp:{current_xp}/{next_level_xp}."
        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)
```

```python
import re

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    extracted_info = []

    # Extract agent's absolute position
    agent_x, agent_y = None, None
    # Use re.DOTALL to allow '.' to match newlines, and \s* for flexible whitespace matching
    agent_pos_match = re.search(r"cursor:\s*(?:Yourself a archeologist)?\s*\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    if agent_pos_match:
        agent_x = int(agent_pos_match.group(1))
        agent_y = int(agent_pos_match.group(2))
        extracted_info.append(f"Agent_absolute_position: ({agent_x}, {agent_y}).")

    # Extract map information if agent position is known
    if agent_x is not None and agent_y is not None:
        map_start_idx = observation_text.find("map:\n")
        if map_start_idx != -1: # Ensure map section exists
            map_content_after_tag = observation_text[map_start_idx + len("map:\n"):]

            # Define markers for the end of the map content
            map_end_marker_1 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nAgent the Digger")
            map_end_marker_2 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nSt:") # Fallback for stat line

            map_end_offset = -1
            if map_end_marker_1 != -1 and (map_end_marker_2 == -1 or map_end_marker_1 < map_end_marker_2):
                map_end_offset = map_end_marker_1
            elif map_end_marker_2 != -1:
                map_end_offset = map_end_marker_2
            
            map_section_raw = ""
            if map_end_offset != -1:
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag[:map_end_offset].strip('\n')
            else:
                # If no clear end marker like "Agent the Digger" or "St:",
                # take the rest of the content after "map:", assuming it's all map and stripping newlines.
                map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag.strip('\n')

            if map_section_raw:
                map_lines = map_section_raw.split('\n')
                
                # Find the agent symbol '@' on the parsed map_lines (which now include original padding)
                visual_agent_x_on_map, visual_agent_y_on_map = -1, -1
                for r_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                    if '@' in line:
                        visual_agent_x_on_map = line.find('@')
                        visual_agent_y_on_map = r_idx
                        break
                
                if visual_agent_x_on_map != -1 and visual_agent_y_on_map != -1:
                    # Calculate the offset between visual coordinates and absolute coordinates
                    offset_x = agent_x - visual_agent_x_on_map
                    offset_y = agent_y - visual_agent_y_on_map

                    walls = []
                    open_floor = []
                    molten_lava = []
                    fountains = []
                    stairs_up = []
                    
                    for y_offset, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                        for x_offset, char in enumerate(line):
                            abs_x = x_offset + offset_x
                            abs_y = y_offset + offset_y

                            if char == '|':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'vertical'))
                            elif char == '-':
                                walls.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'horizontal'))
                            elif char == '.':
                                open_floor.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '}': # Classified as molten lava based on world knowledge and policy
                                molten_lava.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == 'F': # Explicitly identify 'F' as fountains
                                fountains.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            elif char == '<': # Explicitly identify '<' as stairs up
                                stairs_up.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            # Add more symbols and their interpretations as needed

                    if walls:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Walls observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(walls)))}.")
                    if open_floor:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Open floor observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(open_floor)))}.")
                    if molten_lava: # Report molten lava
                        extracted_info.append(f"Molten lava (lethal, impassable) observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(molten_lava)))}.")
                    if fountains:    # Report fountains
                        extracted_info.append(f"Fountains observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(fountains)))}.")
                    if stairs_up:    # Report stairs up
                        extracted_info.append(f"Stairs up observed at (absolute coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_up)))}.")

    # Extract inventory items and general game messages
    message_content_raw = ""
    message_match = re.search(r"message:\n([\s\S]+?)(?=\n\ncursor:|\n\nmap:|$)", observation_text)
    if message_match:
        message_content_raw = message_match.group(1)
        
    inventory_items = []
    general_messages_content = []
    
    # Process message content line by line to differentiate items from general messages
    initial_message_lines = message_content_raw.strip().split('\n')
    
    # Pattern to find individual inventory items on a line or within a line, separated by '.  '
    # Captures: 1: item_key (char), 2: item_description (text without trailing dot)
    # This regex is designed to extract a key and its description, stopping before the next period that
    # either marks the end of the item or precedes another item on the same line (separated by '  ').
    item_pattern_on_line = re.compile(r"([a-z]) - ([^.]+?)(?=\.  |\.($|\s*\n))") # The `($|\s*\n)` handles end of content or end of line.

    for line in initial_message_lines:
        line_contains_items = False
        potential_items_in_line = []
        
        # Find all individual item matches within the current line
        for match in item_pattern_on_line.finditer(line):
            item_key = match.group(1)
            item_description = match.group(2).strip()
            potential_items_in_line.append(f"Inventory item '{item_key}': {item_description}.")
            line_contains_items = True 
        
        if line_contains_items:
            # If any items were found on the line, append them to the inventory list.
            inventory_items.extend(potential_items_in_in_line) # Fixed: typo potential_items_in_in_line to potential_items_in_line
        else:
            # If no inventory items were found on this specific line, consider it part of the general message.
            general_messages_content.append(line)

    cleaned_general_message = '\n'.join([l.strip() for l in general_messages_content if l.strip()])

    if inventory_items:
        extracted_info.extend(inventory_items)

    if cleaned_general_message and not cleaned_general_message.startswith("cursor:"): # Filter out cursor info which is extracted separately
        # Filter out messages that are too short/just punctuation, or contain stat line info (which are extracted separately)
        if (len(cleaned_general_message) > 3 or not re.match(r"^[. ]+$", cleaned_general_message)) and \
           not re.search(r"Dlvl:\d+.*Xp:\d+/\d+", cleaned_general_message) and \
           not re.search(r"St:\d+ Dx:\d+ Co:\d+ In:\d+ Wi:\d+ Ch:\d+ Lawful S:\d+", cleaned_general_message):
            extracted_info.append(f"Game_message: {cleaned_general_message}.")

    # Extract agent stats
    stats_match = re.search(
        r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",
        observation_text
    )
    if stats_match:
        dlvl, gold, current_hp, max_hp, current_pw, max_pw, ac, current_xp, next_level_xp = stats_match.groups()
        extracted_info.append(
            f"Agent_stats: Dlvl:{dlvl}, Gold:{gold}, HP:{current_hp}/{max_hp}, Pw:{current_pw}/{max_pw}, AC:{ac}, Xp:{current_xp}/{next_level_xp}."
        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)
```
